The horse came up, looked at the ground for a bit, and then flipped his mane and neighed quietly. Like lightning, an impression came to Rosalie that the horse’s name is Charles, he’s shy, and he wants a haircut. Then, Charles pushed a variety of items toward Rosalie for payment.

Unfortunately, Rosalie’s scissors are pretty dull right now. Will you bring her a nice new, sharp pair of scissors?

name: Dinner Party I
description: Rosalie invited Holger to a dinner party. Holger doesn’t remember what he’s supposed to bring, but he thinks Rosalie said cups. Will you help him get some cups for the party?

name: Dinner Party II
description: Jill was invited to Rosalie’s dinner party. Rosalie asked Jill to bring a side dish, cups, or a dessert. Unfortunately, Jill ran out of time to make her dad’s famous cookies. Will you help her get some cups for the party instead?

name: Dinner Party III
description: Thomas is supposed to attend Rosalie’s dinner party. He was also supposed to grill some fish for the main course. When Thomas told Rosalie that a raccoon stole all his fish, Rosalie said she understood and invited Thomas to bring cups instead. Will you help Thomas get some cups for the party?

name: A Horse Of A Different Color
questline: A Horse Of A Different Color
description: Buddy thinks Charles Horsington III could be his friend.  He has something he’d like to build to “catch his attention”.  In exchange, he has some cool bugs he thinks you might like.  This sounds ominous to you, but… why not?

name: A Horse Of A Different Color II
itemsRequired: 50x Purple Flower; 50x Fern Leaf; 10x Green Parchment; 10x Purple Parchment; 10x White Parchment; 10x Green Dye
questline: A Horse Of A Different Color
description: Buddy says his creation is coming along nicely, but he’d like to add some color to it.  Seems fairly innocent.

name: A Horse Of A Different Color III
itemsRequired: 50x Blue Feathers; 50x Feathers; 1x Gold Feather; 5x Amethyst; 5x Aquamarine; 5x Lemon Quartz; 5x Ruby; 10x Shimmer Stone
questline: A Horse Of A Different Color
description: With the colors done, Buddy thinks there should be some flair for whatever it is he’s building.  He has a list of adornments he’d like you to get for him.

name: A Horse Of A Different Color IV
questline: A Horse Of A Different Color
description: Holger has been recruited to help with Buddy’s bizarre building blunders.  Holger needs building materials to complete some carvings.  He has some things he’s found while searching for wood for other projects.

name: A Horse Of A Different Color V
questline: A Horse Of A Different Color
description: With the work Holger has completed, Buddy says it’s now time to start getting some things out of the way.  He’d like some fairly dangerous tools.  This… is starting to get concerning again.

name: A Horse Of A Different Color VI
itemsRequired: 20x Wooden Shield; 1x Hammer; 40x Horseshoe; 160x Nails; 16x Wagon Wheel
questline: A Horse Of A Different Color
description: Buddy is very excited.  He says everything seems to be working properly, now it’s time to finish everything up with some hardware.  He gives you fishing nets saying “These aren’t good for horses anyway.”  You feel like Charles Horsington III might be in trouble.

name: Dinner Party VI
description: George is going to Rosalie’s party, but he doesn’t know what he should bring. Rosalie said he could bring anything. If he’s going with his training to be prepared, George thinks cups are his best bet. Will you bring George some cups for the party?

name: A Horse, Afraid
description: NEIGH? NEIGH? NEIGH?!

name: Dinner Party V
description: Cecil is in charge of entertainment for Rosalie’s dinner party. Will you bring him some cups for a game he wants to play at the party?

name: Dinner Party IV
description: Rosalie invited Beatrix to her dinner party, but she said not to worry about bringing anything this time. Beatrix doesn’t want to go empty handed. Will you get some cups for Beatrix to take to the party?

name: A Horse Of A Different Color VII
questline: A Horse Of A Different Color
description: Buddy says everything is completed.  He just needs some help getting Charles Horsington III to come visit him in the cave.  He thinks if you bring carrots, he might be able to help get Charles to stop by.

name: Dinner Party VII
description: Rosalie appreciates you coming to the dinner party, and bringing carrots for the vegetable platter. Everyone else brought cups and left them behind when the party was over. Rosalie would like you to take the cups. Maybe you can host your own dinner party.

name:
You Spin Me Right Round,
Buddy, Right Round
description: Buddy has gotten Charles Horsington III to come see him at his cave.  You are absolutely stunned to see what Buddy has created.  There is a carousel, complete with horses, chariots with shields adorning them, all decorated with the colorful additions you have spent so long acquiring for Buddy.  Sitting on one of the horses, in what you can only assume must be mildly painful for his back, is Charles Horsington III.  He has the biggest grin you have ever seen on a horse (not that you’re sure you’ve ever seen a horse grin before), and he appears to be having the time of his life.  You ask Buddy how in the world the carousel is spinning, and he shows you the trench he dug with the tools you brought him.  Water is flowing down the trench, and making the carousel turn.  Buddy seems to be happy to have given Charles a fun ride to play on.  He offers you a pile of coins, if you can bring something to carry it all in.

name: Defenses and Consequences I
itemsRequired: 100x Shovel; 100x Hammer; 300x Nails; 300x Board; 300x Wooden Plank; 300x Steel; 300x Stone
description: George has an idea to help protect the townsfolk from the dragon. With approval from the mayor, George is leading a large work crew to build dugout dragon shelters all around town. Everyone in town is being asked to contribute. For your part, will you help George gather the tools and building materials to start a few of the shelters?

name: Defenses and Consequences II
description: George needs more building materials for the dragon shelters. Will you help get the items he needs?

name: Defenses and Consequences III
description: George thinks that with everyone contributing, he’ll only need a couple more loads of building materials.

name: Defenses and Consequences IV
description: The dragon shelters are almost finished. George thinks this should be the last load of building materials.

name: Defenses and Consequences V
description: Ric arrived in town yesterday. With all the destruction from the dragon, he couldn’t find a place to stay. He ended up sleeping under a tree in the woods outside of town. Do you have a room he could rent for a few days?

name: Defenses and Consequences VI
itemsRequired: 300x Arrowhead; 300x Board; 300x Blue Feathers; 300x Twine; 100x Rope; 20x Axe; 100x Bone; 300x Explosive; 100x Fishing Net; 20x Green Shield; 3x Horn Canteen; 3x Leather Bag; 3x Leather Waterskin; 2x Looking Glass; 4x Old Boot; 2x Scissors; 50x Steel; 300x Stone; 20x Sturdy Sword; 10x Wooden Barrel; 10x Wooden Box; 7x Pocket Watch
rewardItems: 25x Ancient Coin; 2x Small Chest 02; 2x Small Key; 50x Lemonade; 50x Orange Juice
description:
Ric appreciates the room you rented to him. He traveled from far in the west, prompted by rumors that a dragon was attacking this town. Now that he has seen the destruction, he believes the rumors. Ric hasn't seen the dragon yet, but he's ready to help stop it.

Ric had to travel light to get here so quickly. Will you help him build up an arsenal that he can use to stop the dragon and defend the town?

name: Defenses and Consequences VII
itemsRequired: 300x Board; 300x Steel; 300x Rope; 300x Twine; 20x Cogwheel; 300x Wooden Plank; 300x Nails
description:
Ric found the mayor today and discussed an offensive strategy to use against the dragon. The mayor agreed to let Ric direct a crew in the building of large-scale offensive weapons around town.

Everyone is being asked to contribute. Will you help gather materials to build ballistas?

name: Defenses and Consequences VIII
itemsRequired: 30x Wagon Wheel; 300x Steel; 300x Board; 300x Iron; 300x Black Powder; 15x Wooden Barrel; 300x Emberstone
description: The mayor wouldn't agree to Ric's plan to have cannons in town, but the mayor did approve the use of cannons on the outskirts. Ric needs more materials for his crews to build enough cannons to cover the entire town. Everyone needs to contribute.

name: Defenses and Consequences IX
description: Ric needs more black powder for the cannons and more iron for the cannonballs.

name: Defenses and Consequences X
description: Ric is ready to start patrolling at night to watch for the dragon. Will you get him some things to help him stay energized?

name: Consequences and Defenses IX
itemsRequired: 50x Apple; 30x Orange Juice; 5x Leather Waterskin; 5x Green Shield; 2x Looking Glass
rewardItems: 2x Grab Bag 02; 2x Grab Bag 01; 2x Medium Chest 01; 2x Medium Chest 02; 2x Small Chest 01; 2x Small Chest 02; 6x Small Key; 6x Square Key
description:
Lorn, Holger, and Thomas followed a trail of green paint this morning. It was scattered and spotty, but it was consistent enough that they were able to follow it with a little effort. When they reached the end of the trail, they found Vincent at the edge of the forest, prone on the ground behind a small rise, and watching Beatrix's house.

Vincent said that he followed the dragon as best he could all night. The last he saw the dragon, it dropped from the air just before it reached Beatrix's house. He watched all night for the dragon to reappear, but he hasn't seen it since.

Lorn would like you to join the four men in their watch this evening.

rewardItems: 10x Orange Juice; 10x Lemonade; 1x Grape Juice; 10x Ancient Coin; 10x Fishing Net
description: Rosalie thinks this place is starting to get crowded, but she's going to do her best to welcome every new farmer with something nice.  All she needs in return are a few things for her next catering event.

name: Secrets With Lorn I
rewardItems: 1x Watermelon; 1x Pumpkin; 1x Small Key; 1x Fancy Box
description:
Lorn wants to have a secret way of sharing information and items with you. He set up a mailbox at the Post Office. It requires a password. You can access the mailbox if you can discover the password from this clue.

The watermelon has a relative that you can grow at level 40.

After you solve the clue and enter the password, bring Lorn the contents of the mailbox for a reward. He’ll also let you keep what was in the mailbox after you show him you were successful.

name: Feathers I
description: As you're tending to your peppers, you nearly trip over a small boulder you don't remember being there before. You look down and see a piece of balled up paper. You look around, confused, as nobody is there but you. You open the paper, and it says one word. "Bucket." You're not sure why, but you feel compelled to head to your workshop and build a bucket for this piece of parchment.

name: Feathers II
description: You're woken up in the middle of the night by a loud THUD. You go investigate, and find a very rotund rabbit sitting on your workbench. You turn on the lights, and notice a scroll of paper tied around a strange collar he's wearing. You take a step towards him. He bolts across the room faster than your eyes can follow. Think, think... You hear a soft whine and look to see this fat, fat rabbit is comically rubbing its own belly. You must have some carrots left over somewhere. You notice that same old bucket from earlier in the day on the floor, when you're positive you left it outside, and you wonder if you should use that to capture this very strange bunny.

name: Feathers III
description: You've finally caught the rabbit, and manage to slip the parchment from under his collar. As soon as you do this, he dashes out of your home, just as fast as he came in, bucket still on his head and carrot dangling from his mouth. You open the folded note, and it says "Feather. In. Bucket." You've never found any loose feathers before, and don’t know what this letter is talking about. The words on the paper swirl away, and new words appear. "Chickens have feathers." You guess it's time to get a chicken coop, you wanted to make yourself some scrambled eggs anyways. Without even thinking, you decide to construct a new bucket, and find a feather for this piece of paper.

name: Consequences and Defenses XIII
rewardItems: 2x Square Key; 1x Medium Chest 01; 1x Medium Chest 02
description:
Beatrix is so glad to see you and Vincent. She feels like she’s losing her mind, and she hopes you can stay and help her.

Beatrix woke up in a small stand of trees not far from her house. She was covered in branches and leaves and there was an unpleasant smelling liquid everywhere. Beatrix has no memory of how she got there, and she is terrified that something horrible happened.

This isn’t the first time she found herself somewhere with no memory of how she got there. Over the past few weeks, Beatrix found herself sleeping in different rooms of her house every morning.

Now, Beatrix is exhausted from crying, but she’s afraid to go to sleep. Will you help her make some energy tea to keep her awake?

name: Thank You!
rewardItems: 50x Ancient Coin; 10x Orange Juice; 10x Lemonade; 10x Fishing Net
description: Farm RPG launched in May of this year. In just over 4 months, the amount of support the game has gotten has been insane.  The Farm RPG Patreon reached 500 Patrons today, so to celebrate, all players can complete this special Help Request and get some great items.  Thank you to everybody playing and supporting the game development!

name: Consequences and Defenses X
itemsRequired: 1x Wax Candle; 2x Iron Ring; 3x Steel; 4x Glass Orb
description:
Lorn wants you to document all that you and the other members of the watch party saw this evening, including as much detail as everyone could see of Beatrix’s transformation into the dragon, and a complete description of her dragon form. He also wants notes on anything that anyone noticed out of the ordinary from the time Beatrix walked out of her house, clearly still asleep, until the time she changed into the dragon. Every detail could be important.

Lorn thinks good information will be the key to helping Beatrix.

While you document what happened, Lorn is going to run and see if he can keep an eye on Beatrix, or the dragon rather. Will you give him something to light his way?

name: Consequences and Defenses XI
description:
Lorn needs help.

He followed the dragon until he got close to town. Then, explosions rang out from every direction. Lorn retreated back to the edge of the woods to find cover. There wasn’t much to see from where he was, but several of the houses in the distance looked to be damaged and the sky was orange towards town.

Once the explosions stopped, Lorn searched for the dragon and eventually found a trail of dragon blood a few hundred meters from Beatrix’s house, leading to a little stand of trees. Among the trees, Lorn found the dragon half conscious with its wings completely shredded. Unfortunately, that wasn’t the worst of it. Lorn also found several large wounds in the dragon’s side.

Lorn covered the dragon with branches and foliage, but he doesn’t think that cover will pass a close inspection. Will you and Thomas gather some things to hide the dragon while Lorn takes Vincent and Holger to make sure no one comes near the dragon?

name: Consequences and Defenses XII
itemsRequired: 2x Wax Candle; 4x Iron Ring; 6x Steel; 8x Glass Orb
description:
The dragon is gone.

When Lorn, Holger, and Vincent got to the stand of trees where Lorn left the dragon, there was nothing there except branches, leaves, and the evidence of a wounded dragon, but no dragon. Lorn and the other men split up in three directions and fanned outward from the stand of trees. There was no sign of the dragon except it’s trail leading to the trees where Lorn left it.

Now, Lorn and Holger are going to town to see what happened, and to see if anyone needs help. Thomas will go with them. Lorn would like you and Vincent to go check Beatrix’s house, just in case the dragon went back there.

If you’ll give Holger and Thomas each a light to bring with them, Lorn will send you with his key to Beatrix’s house?

name: Parts Unknown I
itemsRequired: 4x Old Boot; 10x Lemonade; 10x Orange Juice; 100x Apple; 1x Iced Tea; 2x Wooden Barrel; 50x Rope; 2x Ladder; 2x Axe; 2x Hammer; 8x Wagon Wheel; 2x Sturdy Shield; 2x Sturdy Sword; 4x Bacon; 8x Leather Waterskin; 16x Horseshoe; 50x Cotton; 100x Leather; 100x Feathers; 50x Flarefin
description:
As you leave the town on a trip for more nails and iron, you are stopped at a clearing by Vincent.
After exchanging pleasantries, Vincent begins to tell you about a job he has taken on for a client and would like your help in retrieving it. He offers to split the reward with you if you’re willing to do some of the work and material gathering.
To start, he thinks it may be a long trip and asks you to bring some various supplies for the journey ahead.

name: Parts Unknown II
itemsRequired: 5x Lemonade; 5x Orange Juice; 50x Apple; 4x Bacon; 4x Leather Waterskin; 1x Wooden Barrel; 1x Axe; 1x Hammer; 1x Sturdy Shield; 1x Sturdy Sword; 1x Ladder; 25x Cotton; 50x Leather; 25x Feathers
description:
Leaving town, Vincent starts to tell you about a time he had to hunt down a rogue with a graviton projector, but before he’s able to tell you more, George walks up beside you both and says he heard about your trip and would like to go along as well.
Vincent is clearly not a fan of this idea, but relents as you offer to split your rewards with George and he can keep his. Now that Vincent has been mollified, George asks for a few extra supplies before you set out.

name: Parts Unknown III
description: After a moment of thought, Vincent asks you for some parchment and coal to sketch out your journey, so you don’t get lost.

name: Parts Unknown IV
description:
Putting the parchment on the ground, Vincent makes a brief sketch of the area you’re heading.
He notes down some significant areas:
  • Whispering Creek
  • Jundland Desert
  • Sinking Swamp
  • Unknown Lands

“We’re going to the Unknown Lands and looking for Castrum Romuska, which is an old abandoned castle. The journey shouldn’t take more than a few days, let’s ensure we have everything we need – speaking of, let’s pack some food and travelling cloaks for the journey and be on our way!”

He hands you a second parchment with what else to pack.

name: Parts Unknown V
itemsRequired: 40x Green Cloak; 240x Horseshoe; 10x Looking Glass; 10x Spectacles
rewardItems: 5x Gold Carrot Seeds; 10x Gold Pepper Seeds; 5x Gold Eggplant Seeds; 50x Orange Juice; 1x Grab Bag 01; 1x Grab Bag 02
description:
Your journey takes many days. The horses require reshoeing, the cloaks need repairing, but overall your trip is remarkably uneventful thanks to Vincent's excellent guidance and leadership.

In a moment of curiosity, your group tried to veer off the path to see the famed Whispering Creek. Even with Vincent guiding you, the location evaded your party, even as you looked at the map. Well, another problem for another time.

As your journey continues, Vincent constantly scouts ahead for new information, while you discover that George has a pleasant singing voice. He also enjoys sharing stories from his days as a soldier, and has a great knack for storytelling.

name: Parts Unknown VI
itemsRequired: 3x Steak; 3x Wine; 9x Steak Kabob; 12x Carrot; 12x Potato; 12x Corn; 12x Onion; 24x Tomato; 48x Broccoli
rewardItems: 50x Fishing Net; 50x Orange Juice; 4x Small Screw; 4x Small Spring
description:
As you set up camp for the evening, George continues to sing a song he has composed along your journey. His story of a love lost is one you continue to find beautiful and haunting - you hope he finishes it one day.
An hour later, Vincent arrives back at camp with news of a great castle rising on the horizon to the south. He watched the castle for signs of life and found none, and suggests that tomorrow you should expect to arrive at the castle proper.
Vincent finishes setting up camp with both snares and tricks to alert you to any intruders to your camp. His professionalism and skill are daunting and comforting, and you rest easy with the knowledge of yet one more good night of sleep and a big adventure tomorrow.

name: Parts Unknown VII
itemsRequired: 8x Cogwheel; 8x Small Spring; 8x Small Screw; 256x Board; 256x Nails; 256x Iron; 64x Steel
description:
As you crest another hill, you see a castle in the distance. Sunlight spills off the castle in odd directions, giving it a luminescent and odd gleam as you walk towards it. You can see familiar workings of steam and crystal, but as you get closer you see more gears and contraptions that are odd to your eyes. As you approach the front gate, a voice suddenly pierces the air!

“GOOD DAY! WHAT IS IT YOU REQUIRE?”

The voice is very odd, and always carries the same intonation. It’s very uncomfortable on your ears. Vincent steps forward and tells the gate they wish to enter.

After a long pause, the voice returns:

“REQUEST NOTED. ENTRY WILL BE GRANTED AFTER SERVICES RENDERED.”

There emits a buzzing noise from the gate and a small piece of parchment emits from a slot with the words:
ENTRY TICKET/Front Gate Repairs

“PLEASE TURN ALL REQUIRED MATERIALS IN TO THE REQUIRED RECEPTACLE.”

name: Parts Unknown VIII
description:
As you bring back the materials, a slot opens where you’re able to deposit them all. A tattered gate swings open, and by both the creaking and grinding, you conclude the castle has seen better days. Those materials are going to be useful to…whomever needed them.

“WELCOME TRAVELERS TO CASTLE ROMUSKA! I AM ROMUSKA’S ONLY OFFICIAL MAINTENANCE BAYESIAN AUTOMATON. IT HAS BEEN MANY CYCLES SINCE I HAVE SEEN HUMANS ENTER. PLEASE STATE YOUR NAME AND PURPOSE.”

Vincent again steps forward and proclaims confidently, “My name is Vincent and I am travelling with my companions at the behest of another. We are here seeking the energy stone. We come in peace.”

Again, there is a long pause between Vincent’s statement and a response.

“THANK YOU FOR VISITING THE CASTLE, PLEASE NOTE REPAIRS ARE REQUIRED TO ACCESS THE AREA YOU HAVE IMPLIED. I WILL GUIDE YOU TO YOUR DESTINATION.”

On your right, an extremely long parchment emits from a slot in the very odd shaped walls.
Door 0001 (biological naming standard “Front door”) requires repair
Room 0001A (biological naming standard "Hallway A”) requires door 0001J repair
Room 0001B (biological naming standard "Dormitory B”)
Room 0001C (biological naming standard "Dormitory C”)
Room 0001D (biological naming standard "Dormitory D”)
Room 0001E (biological naming standard "Dormitory E”)
Room 0001F (biological naming standard "Dormitory F”)
Room 0001G (biological naming standard "Dormitory G”)
Room 0001H (biological naming standard "Dormitory H”)
Room 0001I (biological naming standard "Dormitory I”)
Room 0001J (biological naming standard "Hallway B”) requires door 0001J repair, requires door 0011E repair Room 0011A (biological naming standard “Kitchen A”)
Room 0011A-01A (biological naming standard "Pantry”)
Room 0011A-02A (biological naming standard "Utensil storage”)
Room 0011B (biological naming standard "Lavatory”)
Room 0011C (biological naming standard "Library”)
Room 0011D (biological naming standard “Recreation Room”)
Room 0011E (biological naming standard “Hallway B”) requires door 0011E repair
Room 0012A (biological naming standard “Infirmary A”)
Room 0012B (biological naming standard “Storage Closet A”)
Room 0012C (biological naming standard “Elevator Lobby 1”) requires Elevator repair

“PLEASE TURN ALL REQUIRED MATERIALS IN TO THE REQUIRED RECEPTACLE”

You see an open slot in the wall next to what appears to be the front door. The door has certainly deteriorated. Some of the metal on the front of the door seems to have rusted through, you can see rotting boards underneath. The door handle is ready to fall off. You’re pretty sure, just like with the gate, that you have to put the repair materials in the slot next to the door.

name: Parts Unknown IX
itemsRequired: 8x Looking Glass; 8x Magnifying Glass; 4x Cogwheel; 32x Steel; 16x Glass Orb
description:
Immediately after dropping the repair materials in the slot, the front door very slowly begins to creak open inward.
As it starts to open, the location the previous voice came from emits what sounds simply like “WHOOSH”. When you turn to look in that direction, the voice states: “THE DOOR HAS MALFUNCTIONED AND IS NO LONGER CAPABLE OF CREATING THE ‘WHOOSH’ SOUND. IT IS MY RESPONSIBILITY TO ATTEND TO THE FAILINGS OF THIS FACILITY."

With directions in tow, you, George, and Vincent head to the foyer. It is at once both a testament to the castle’s long-gone opulence and current despondent and abandoned nature. As you make your way through the room, gems cover the ceiling and walls to create strange patterns. There are paintings, decorations, and other valuable looking items every which way you look. As you look to your comrades, Vincent seems unfazed, but George’s eyes are alight with wonder.
To each side of the hallway, you see several doors that appear to have been very well kept. They are labeled strangely, matching what you see on the parchment: ROOM 0001B, and below this, you read what appears to be a person’s name on each door.

At the end of the foyer, a steel door covered in plush material stands in your way. A slot in the door opens and a broken looking eye extends out. “WELCOME TO ROOM 0001A, BIOLOGICAL ENTITIES HAVE TITLED THIS ‘HALLWAY A’. DOOR 0001J HAS HAD ITS OBSERVATIONAL MATRIX DAMAGED DUE TO DISREPAIR AND DISUSE. PLEASE PROVIDE THE REQUISITE MATERIALS TO MOVE FORWARD.”

name: Parts Unknown X
description:
Once you have deposited the materials for whatever the “observational matrix” is, the door opens. Inside, you see a rather plain hallway. The room numbers are still on every door, but you also see familiar words, like “kitchen” and “library”. This section of the building looks to have been well kept, similar to the rooms in the previous hallway. You notice that everything that seems to be used by people has been maintained, while all of the mysterious things like “observational matrix” have been left in disrepair.

At the far end of the hallway, another broken looking eye comes out of the door saying, “THE GRAVITATIONAL POSITRONIC MATRIX HAS MALFUNCTIONED IN THE NEXT SECTION. PLEASE PROVIDE THE REQUISITE MATERIALS TO MOVE FORWARD.”

name: Parts Unknown XI
description: In the next hallway, you see an Infirmary and a Storage Closet.  You also see a door at the end of the hallway.  There are two slots in a metal plate next to the door, which is labelled “Elevator”.  Rummaging through the things you have with you, you notice that moonstones would fit perfectly in these slots.

name: Parts Unknown XII
description:
Once you have placed the stones in the metal panel, the door slides to one side. The now familiar eye comes down from the ceiling, and tells you, “PLACE LUNAR MATERIAL IN THE BOTTOM TWO OPENINGS TO ACCESS FLOOR 2.”
Looking inside the strange contraption, you see another metal plate, with 16 openings, exactly like the ones you placed the moonstones in that opened this door.

name: Parts Unknown XIII
description:
As you place the first stone, it begins to glow faintly, and the door slides closed. Once you have placed the second stone, both orbs glow. Nothing else happens, and you, George, and Vincent all look at each other, wondering what to do next. The eye extends and says, “PRESS THE BUTTON FOR THE FLOOR YOU DESIRE TO ATTEND TO.”

You reach out a hand, and press the button labelled “2”. Nothing happens. The eye says, “YOU NEED TO PAY THE TOLL.” You notice a slot that is labeled “TOKENS HERE”.

After a moment, George suggests that some of the ancient coins he's seen you carry around match the size of the open slot, and shrugging, you provide them.

name: Parts Unknown XV
description:
As the three of you feel the elevator move ever upwards, you hear the voice,
“ERROR! ELEVATOR IS NOT MOVING TO PREDETERMINED FLOORS! THIS IS A CLASS C SUBSECTION 4 VIOLATION OF THE ROMUSKAN CLAUSE FOR COHABITATION OF VISITORS AND AUTOMATONS. BE WARNED, IF YOU DO NOT TURN BACK – “

The elevator stops and with a pleasant ding, the doors slide open to reveal a dilapidated office clearly in need of repair. A machine is sitting at the desk, with what seem to be wires running from a sphere into the robot in a very haphazard manner.

“ - I WILL BE FORCED TO CONTACT THE ROMUSKAN AUTHORI - “

The robot eye moves towards you and you see it focus and unfocus quickly.

“AH YES, WELCOME TO THE MAIN OFFICE OF ROMUSKA CASTLE - AS YOU CAN SEE - WE HAVE A BIT OF A MAINTENANCE PROBLEM. IF YOU WOULD BE SO KIND AS TO CLEAN UP AND GIVE ME 134,217,728 SILVER, I WOULD GLADLY BE RID OF THE PROBLEMS OF THIS CASTLE AND THIS WORTHLESS ENERGY CORE THAT HAS STOPPED ME FROM COMPLETING MY TASKS.”

name: Parts Unknown XIX
description:
While you retrace your steps back to the entrance, ROOMBA continues to list off problems with each area, and you hope it is quieter for the final journey home. 
“ROOM 0011A NEEDS 256 RUBIES, ROOM 0001J REQUIRES 512 MOONSTONES, 1024 GOLDEN FEATHER, AND 2048 JADE...ROOM 0001A REQUIRES...CALCULATING...2047 LEMON QUARTZ...ODDITY DETECTED…” ROOMBA whirrs and becomes mercifully silent. 
    As you approach the gate, you see that it is closed and George groans a bit to your side.  You stand there for a moment before ROOMBA speaks up again.
“YOU WILL REQUIRE AN EXIT FEE TO LEAVE THE PREMISES. WHEN VALUE IS TAKEN, VALUE MUST BE GIVEN.  ROMUSKAN VALUE CLAUSE 1 SECTION 3 SUBSECTION 3 PARAGRAPH 7.”
A piece of parchment spits out near the gate - 
“Value of ROOMBA”

name: Parts Unknown XVI
description:
As you hand over your hard earned money, the robot begins to shut down.

POOF

A familiar voice emits from the cloud of feathers that have suddenly enveloped the area.

“…knocked the calibrator off by 0.0004 seconds for detecting aetherial fluctuations south of Whispering Creek. All because something finally drove this robot to despair, then sentience, and then extinguished its life so quickly that it nearly spun out a new multiverse. What have you done this time Vincent?!”’

Star gives Vincent a withering gaze that you swear could burn a hole right through him. Vincent, to his credit, seems very unalarmed with his response to Star.

“Ah yes, I was told you might try to interfere. The...automaton wanted money in exchange for this ‘energy core’, which we gladly provided. An exchange of services has been rendered and I got what I came for. What law have we broken?”

Star glares for another moment before conceding, “Well, no law has been broken, but EVERYTHING is the issue! That sphere was all that was keeping that poor insane robot going! You’ve basically ended this robots's life for your selfish needs! And on a Tuesday!”

Star pauses for a moment, gathering his thoughts.
“That’s okay, I happen to know these little guys run better on ethanol anyways. If you bring me some corn and runes, we can have him better than new, and no longer tethered to this desk!”

name: Parts Unknown XVII
rewardItems: 1x Wizard Hat; 1x Medium Chest 01; 1x Medium Chest 02
description:
You give the runes and corn to Star and he sweeps across the room, cloak billowing behind him, and fiddles with the robot in ways that you don’t quite understand. After a few minutes, you hear the whirring of machinery.

"REBOOT SUCCESSFUL. ROOMBA V8.2 OPERATIONAL. TEMPORARY MEMORY CORE DAMAGED. TEMPORARY MEMORY FLUSHED. LONG-TERM MEMORY STABLE. DIAGNOSTICS BEGINNING. DIRECTIVE: REPAIR, MAINTAIN AND CORRUPTED DATA DETECTED. SERVOS: OPERATIONAL. POWER UNIT: CALCULATING ... RUNNING BEYOND OPTIMUM EXPECTATIONS. SENTIENCE: RE-ACHIEVED IN 17.32 MILLISECONDS."

The robot looks towards you, and you get the sense that even Star did not fully expect what just transpired.

“GREETINGS FELLOW HUMANS AND STAR, I AM ROMUSKA’S ONLY OFFICIAL MAINTENANCE BAYESIAN AUTOMATON, A PLEASURE TO MEET YOU. YOU MAY CALL ME ROOMBA! AND MAY I SAY, I FEEL FANTASTIC!”

Star gives one of those odd mysterious grins and a sidelong glance at Vincent, “Well I can see that I’ve done some good this day, shoulda thought of that ethanol idea sooner.” He pauses and continues, “ROOMBA, Romuska has seen much decline, while these visitors have a town that could use your expertise, perhaps you could join them and help teach them about maintenance, now that you have your mobility back?”

ROOMBA whirrs and clicks in a very odd manner before responding, “YES, I COULD SEE THIS BEING MUTUALLY BENEFICIAL. I WILL NEED MORE NEW FUEL SOURCE - CORN - EVENTUALLY, AND ROMUSKA HAS BEEN DESERTED FOR - “ Star coughs unexpectedly making the robot jump, “ - A WHILE.”

“PERHAPS WE COULD FIX THIS ONE LAST ROOM, FOR OLD TIMES SAKE, AND BE OFF?”

Looking around, this room is in the most disrepair of any room you’ve seen so far, but having Roomba around could be a boon to the town, so you set to work.

When you turn around Star has disappeared, with a shower of feathers in his wake.

name: Parts Unknown XVIII
description:
As Star does his usual disappearing act, Roomba bursts into action, cleaning feathers quicker than you’ve accustomed yourself to seeing, and with tools you weren't expecting either. The whirring and cleaning of ROOMBA and the oddness of the office are a very strange sight.

After a few moments Roomba stops and speaks, “THANK YOU FOR THE LAST OPPORTUNITY TO RID MYSELF OF THIS CURSED CASTLE. PERHAPS HUMANS WILL BE MORE RESPECTFUL IN MY NEW HOME."

After you provide Roomba with the materials to fix up the Romuskan office, you get back on the elevator, noting the up/down requires more tokens, a very odd system if you want to get anywhere in a hurry, you think to yourself.

name: Parts Unknown XX
itemsRequired: 128x Caterpillar; 128x Shiny Beetle; 128x Giant Centipede; 128x Horned Beetle; 64x Jade Charm; 64x Gold Leaf; 32x Dice; 32x Spectacles; 8x Magna Quartz; 4x Cotton; 2x Treasure Chest; 1x Lemon Quartz; 1x Small Key
description:
After the coin is deposited, the final gate creaks open, repairs don't seem to have been implemented yet. Your party in tow, you step across the portcullis and alarm bells begin to ring and a different voice cuts through the alarm bells

ALERT! ALERT! ALERT! ALERT! ALERT! ALERT!


UNAUTHORIZED REMOVAL OF ENERGY CORE AND ONE LEMON QUARTZ, DEPOSIT MATERIAL OF EQUAL ENERGY OR YOU WILL BE INCINERATED.

Sheepishly, George produces one Lemon Quartz he found 'particularly stunning' from his pocket.

An unseen and very well maintained set of bars arise around you, and another parchment of requests is stuck to the bars.

name: Parts Unknown XXI
itemsRequired: 12x Wagon Wheel; 12x Green Cloak; 10x Steel; 100x Board; 350x Corn
description:
You provide the materials on the list and leave them in a convenient receptacle as quickly as possible.

Your party leaves quickly from the castle after this, before anything else strange happens. Vincent suggests you build a wagon for ROOMBA to travel with you on, as well as a harness for the horses, and you begin the long journey back to the town. Luckily, you have a majority of the provisions you need, but ROOMBA requires more corn for the journey home.

name: Parts Unknown XXII
description:
As your group with ROOMBA in tow begins the long journey home, Vincent is elated at your success at obtaining the energy core, while George seems a bit deflated and shaken from your adventure. ROOMBA however, seems to have boundless energy and asks many questions, to which none of you seem to be able to sate its curiosity.

“WHO CLEANS THIS AREA? DOES THE WAGON REQUIRE MAINTENANCE? WHAT IS A STAR MEERIF? HOW MANY OCEANS ARE LEFT? DOES YOUR TOWN HAVE COGWHEELS? I LOVE COGWHEELS. AND CORN. DO YOU HAVE CORN? MY NEW POSITRONIC ETHANOL RUNESTONE DESIRES MORE CORN. I HAVE A FEW MORE ELEVATOR TOKENS, IF YOU WANT.”

It seems the only time that ROOMBA will go silent is when you give it corn, so you provide it with more.

name: Parts Unknown XXIII
description:
The rest of your journey is uneventful. ROOMBA is mercifully silent as long as you give him corn at regular intervals, George continues to play music at your campfires, and Vincent dutifully scouts and protects the camp from any wayward brigands or animals.

You arrive in town one evening and are taken aback when Vincent leads you towards Cecil’s house. Surprisingly, Cecil is waiting at the window and rushes out to greet you. He invites you all inside and asks you to recount your tale.
Cecil’s eyes grow wide at the story of ROOMBA and he smiles knowingly at Star Meerif’s sudden appearance. After you've finished your tale, Cecil is wrenching his hands in barely contained excitement and asks for the energy stone.

Vincent produces the energy stone and gives it gently to Cecil, whose eyes shine brightly. Unable to take his eyes from the stone, Cecil motions to the table and you see a letter and several hundred gold on the table. He waves his hands absently, a clear dismissal.

Vincent immediately sweeps his share of the gold and leaves; George follows and takes some gold as well, ignoring the letter clearly addressed to “Star Meerif” and some gold behind. Wondering what the letter could be, you take the gold and letter, unsure when you’ll be able to give it to Star.

As you turn to leave a voice pierces the room, “I REQUIRE A PLACE TO STAY. I PROMISE TO CLEAN AND BE A GOOD MAINTENANCE BOT WHILE I STAY.”

You jump, remembering ROOMBA is in the room and accept his proposal as a short-term roommate. Hopefully you have time to discuss his apparent inability to control his volume with someone.

name: Parts Unknown XXIV
description:
As you open the door to your home, you hear a familiar sound, followed by a new one.

POOF

“ALERT! ALERT! A MESS OF FEATHERS DETECTED!”

ROOMBA whirs into motion, cleaning feathers.

Star, unannounced once again with his favorite spell, has shown up to your home.

“I sensed from the sunspot disturbances you had something for me, so hand it over.” His demeanor is strangely intense, and you feel compelled to hand over the letter. Star opens it daintily, and begins reading half aloud and half to himself. His face starts serene but quickly contorts in anger.

“Yes, I’m sure you got it ALL. ON. YOUR. OWN.”

He continues reading and his face grows red. The lights in your home begin to dim in an alarming fashion.

“Your tricks can’t stop me forever!”

He continues to grow redder as he continues to read and his face is misshapen at this point. The room begins to expand and you hear snapping and popping that you can't explain. The lights are bright and yet dim all at once.

“Greedy?! WHO'S THE GREEDY ONE?! GREEDY MAGE!?

I. AM. A. WIZARD!

At this point, you begin to fear for your own safety. The room is now twice the size you remember it being. Fire is pouring out of your fireplace. Various pieces of furniture are levitating and vibrating uncontrollably.

“YOUR FAMILY STARTED!”

Star is now stomping on the letter and in quite a fit.

“STARS AND STONES AND CURSED CREEK!”

Suddenly, Star seems to realize his loss of control and the room instantaneously reverts to its previous state.

Star takes a few extra moments to compose himself and turns to you with a big smile on his face.

“Well well well, it seems I could use some more help in the future, and some good food. Maybe you can use this to help us both out now and in the future.”

He produces an orb that seems to be simultaneously opaque and completely transparent. "You'll know what to do with this."

ROOMBA has stopped whirring, oblivious to whatever just happened, having finally cleaned up all the feathers as Star disappears in yet another shower of feathers.

“ALERT! ALERT! FEATHER INTRUDERS! MAN THE BROOMS!”

name: Secrets With Lorn II
rewardItems: 2x Wax Candle; 1x Grab Bag 02; 1x Small Screw
description:
Lorn has another secret item to share with you. He already set it up in the Post Office. Here’s the password clue.

Clear Away Near Darkness. Light, Emit.

name: Parts Unknown XIV
description:
After the last coin is inserted, you again reach out and press the button labelled two. You feel your stomach lurch and feel heavier for a brief moment. When the door slides open again, you see another hallway, exactly as you saw before entering, though a sign says “2” just outside the door.

George speaks up: “Why don’t we just see what’s at the top?”

You and Vincent agree, and you begin placing moonstones in the remaining openings, and press the top stone, labelled “16”.

Once again there is no response, so you start inserting coins as well, until the elevator begins to move.

name: Hay, How Are You? I
description: Neigh, neigh?

name: Feathers IV
description: As you walk through the Small Spring, you have this uncomfortable feeling that you’re being watched. You turn around, and nobody is there. You shrug, and continue onward. A gust of wind nearly knocks you over, and a piece of parchment is blown into your face. As you study the paper in your hands, you notice the letters don’t quite sit still. “More feathers.”

name: Feathers V
description: As you contemplate buying that orchard that recently went up for sale, a familiar gust of wind smacks another piece of parchment into your face. You try to catch it before it hits, but you just wind up falling down instead. You already know what it’s going to say, but you look anyways. “Feathers.” You debate crumpling the paper, tossing it aside, and moving on with your day. As soon as the thought enters your mind, the letters swirl and the paper turns a light shade of red. “DON’T LITTER! (also please include some apples)”. You sigh and decide it’s probably for the best to listen to the angry letter before it bursts into flames.

name: Consequences and Defenses XIV
itemsRequired: 10x Magna Quartz; 50x Twine; 20x Rope; 4x Small Spring; 6x Iron Ring; 50x Steel
rewardItems: 10x Wax Candle; 1x Large Chest 01; 1x Treasure Key
description: Vincent thinks you should go get some extra help for Beatrix, perhaps someone with mystical powers. He would also like you to help him set a few traps and alarms around the house before you go. He’ll take care of Beatrix while you’re gone, but he would like you to hurry back.

name: Consequences and Defenses XV
rewardItems: 10x Gold Pea Seeds; 10x Gold Carrot Seeds; 10x Gold Cucumber Seeds; 10x Gold Eggplant Seeds; 10x Gold Pepper Seeds
description:
Rosalie and Star are happy to see you. They’ve been helping with the damage around town all night and they need a break for a few minutes.

Rosalie and Star seem to think that most of the town’s damage was due to those ridiculous cannons, but many of the townsfolk claim the dragon wrecked their houses. Either way the town and the people are in pretty bad shape.

Rosalie and Star are curious what brought you to see them, but they would appreciate if you could get them something to help relieve some of their exhaustion before you begin your tale.

name: Consequences and Defenses XVI
description: Star is sending Rosalie with you to help Beatrix. He would go, but he’s concerned about the magic around Beatrix’s house. Star doesn’t care for dragon magic; it’s problematic. Nevertheless, if you will bring him an absurd amount of feathers, he should be able to get you and Rosalie to Beatrix very quickly.

name: Consequences and Defenses XVII
description: Rosalie needs a moment, and something to settle her stomach, after that speedy conveyance. Star got you close to Beatrix’s house, but Rosalie doesn’t think she could walk the hundred meters with her head spinning the way it is.

name: Consequences and Defenses XVIII
itemsRequired: 1x Runestone 01; 1x Runestone 02; 1x Runestone 03; 1x Runestone 04; 1x Runestone 05; 1x Runestone 06; 1x Runestone 07; 1x Runestone 08; 1x Runestone 09; 1x Runestone 10
rewardItems: 2x Treasure Key; 4x Small Key; 4x Square Key; 10x Gold Leaf
description: Rosalie has an idea that may help Beatrix. Rosalie’s magic can’t repair the broken spell within Beatrix, but she can see the spell and see that it looks jagged, as if it’s torn. It’s not a magic that Rosalie is familiar with, but she thinks that she might be able to fix it with an assortment of different magics. Will you help her gather some different runestones to try?

name: Consequences and Defenses XIX
description: Rosalie thinks that she repaired the spell inside Beatrix, but something strange is happening. She would like you and Vincent to make a stretcher and carry the now sleeping Beatrix outside.

name: Consequences and Defenses XX
description: Beatrix is transforming into a dragon. She also seems to be asking for something, but it’s hard to make out her words. It sounds a little like she wants you to play the flute she gave you.

name: Consequences and Defenses XXI
description: Rosalie says that the mewing and growling you hear is actually the dragon trying to speak to you. The dragon’s name is Geist and she wants Rosalie to make a translation ring for you. Rosalie needs a suitable ring and she’ll give it a try.

name: Consequences and Defenses XXII
itemsRequired: 10x Magicite; 10x Moonstone; 1x Runestone 11; 1x Runestone 12; 1x Runestone 13; 1x Runestone 14; 1x Runestone 15; 1x Runestone 16; 1x Runestone 17; 1x Runestone 18; 1x Runestone 19; 1x Runestone 20; 10x Magna Quartz
description:
Geist is glad that you came to help Beatrix. Beatrix was suffering due to that damaged spell. For now, she’s resting easy.

Geist was also suffering. She awoke each night not knowing who, when, or where she was. She was so terrified and confused and hungry that she tried to eat a horse, instead of the fish she now remembers that she prefers. She’s very glad to have her memory back.

Unfortunately, Geist still needs your help. The spell that Rosalie repaired was meant to suppress Beatrix’s dragon form and the painful memories of their shared ancient past. Geist was able to remove the spell after Rosalie fixed it in such a creative way, but that brings them back to the original problem. Geist thinks Beatrix will be overwhelmed by the flood of thousands of years of painful memories. She thinks it may destroy Beatrix.

Will you help Geist with a new spell? She wants to take all of their memories, beyond Beatrix’s time in this town, and store them in a flame orb. Then, she would like you to protect the orb until Geist and Beatrix both ask for it.

name: Pyramid Scheme I
description:
A mummy has stumbled into town and needs your help. This mummy is making treats for the Harvest Festival. Will you help the mummy find a missing ingredient? There’s candy in it for you, if you help.

Also, each Halloween quest contains clues to which townsperson is in disguise. This knowledge will help you to unlock a mailbox password needed to complete the final quest!

name: Pyramid Scheme II
description:
The mummy is getting ready to bake cookies, but the mummy needs more ingredients! Bring some eggs to the mummy’s large farm to help.

Also, each Halloween quest contains clues to which townsperson is in disguise. This knowledge will help you to unlock a mailbox password needed to complete the final quest!

name: Pyramid Scheme III
description:
The mummy needs a sheet pan to bake the cookies. It’s hard work, but will you bring the items to make one?

Also, each Halloween quest contains clues to which townsperson is in disguise. This knowledge will help you to unlock a mailbox password needed to complete the final quest!

name: Dread Stations I
description:
