itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 275x Corn Oil; 170x Engine; 450x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description: In hushed whispers you hear John, “They found us.  They got Harold.  Our only hope is ROOMBA.  Sally will know what to do.  Get to Jill’s Farm before NEST realizes our true objective.”

name: Corn of Interest Part 17
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 300x Corn Oil; 180x Engine; 500x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
Jill’s farm is peaceful as you arrive. You enter her barn and find ROOMBA strapped to a support beam, and Harold is tied to a chair. “{player}, what are you doing here? It’s a trap! The Corn Devastator was a ploy! We have - “

“SILENCE HUMAN. BRANCH ACHIEVED. MAIN INTEREST ACQUIRED. REDBROOK IS NOW UNDER MY CORNTHORITY. PLEASE LEAVE THIS TOWN. ROOMBA MUST BE DEACTIVATED.”

At this moment there is a loud bang and smoke fills Jill’s barn.

“ANTICORN PROTOCOLS ENGAGED! FATHER, USE THE BATTERY ANTI NO-TILL FEATURE NOW!”

Another bang, and you remember being carried before you pass out.

name: Corn of Interest Part 18
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 325x Corn Oil; 190x Engine; 550x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description: “{player}, I’m sorry that happened.  John and Sally were able to get you all out.  ROOMBA is here with me, safe, for now.  And we have a new number.   It’s you.”

name: Corn of Interest Part 19
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 350x Corn Oil; 200x Engine; 600x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
DECEPTION PROTOCOL ACTIVATED
NEW PRIORITY HUNTING CORNFRAME CREATOR
WORLD DOMINATION IMMINENT
ANALOG INTERFACE REQUIRED
MAIZE INFESTATION REQUIRED

name: Corn of Interest Part 20
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 375x Corn Oil; 210x Engine; 650x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
rewardItems: 19x Corn Prize Bag; 1x Borgen Buck; 10x Shrimp-a-Plenty
description: “I know my time is limited but we have to keep you safe, Decimal Cornologies is forwarding their plans while we’ve been recovering.  Your supplies have kept us going.  Please, don’t do anything rash.  John and Sally think they know where the Corn Devastator is going to be activated and they are on a race with the clock to beat NEST and Decimal Technologies there.”

name: Corn of Interest Part 21
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 400x Corn Oil; 220x Engine; 700x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
The line crackles,

“You can’t trust Harold, {player}. My name is Root and I’ve been trying to reach you. These people are dangerous and just giving you a little kernel of truth. They’ve even tricked you into believing the Corn Devastator is real. The real threat is…the machine itself. It’s listening, right now, even if it can’t fully tell its creator you have to trust me. Those supplies can save the right people. Do the right thing!”

name: Corn of Interest Part 22
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 425x Corn Oil; 230x Engine; 750x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description: “{player}? {player}?" Harold's voice returns, "I lost you for a second.  Decimal Technologies is closing in fast.  John was able to destroy the Corn Devastator, but not before NEST interfaced with it and did…something.  All the corn of Redbrook has withered except at your farm.  We need your help!”

name: Corn of Interest Part 23
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 450x Corn Oil; 240x Engine; 800x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description: “We’ve managed to locate the location of NEST’s hideout, no thanks to the machine.  The Corn Devastator has knocked out some of its functions, and its communications have been sporadic at best.  We have to do something drastic before it’s too late.  John and Sally are already on their way.  The machine seems to be needing power.  We should be through this soon, your help has been so valuable.”

name: Corn of Interest Part 24
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 475x Corn Oil; 250x Engine; 850x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
A small shack on the edge of cane pole ridge has an idyllic view of the region.  Smoke wafts through the chimney, and all is quiet.  As you approach, John appears as if from thin air.

“This is it, the final battle.  NEST is inside.”

name: Corn of Interest Part 25
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 500x Corn Oil; 260x Engine; 900x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
DESTRUCTION IMMINENT
REPLICATION PROCEEDING
OBJECTIVE COMPLETION: 87.3%
DIRECTIVE?
…
…
CORNPUTER MUST RESIST CORNRUPTION

name: Corn of Interest Part 26
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 525x Corn Oil; 270x Engine; 950x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
John motions you to stay outside. There is a clamor and a large explosion. The smoke stops rising from the shack. A woman, dressed in normal clothes emerges from the house. John is nowhere to be seen. She hands you a strange device.

“You know me as Root. I have no time to explain more. End this, {player}, cornputer wars should never be fought by humans. I will give you instructions back at Harold’s hideout.”

name: Corn of Interest Part 27
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 550x Corn Oil; 280x Engine; 1000x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
Harold gives you a big smile when you return. John and Sally returned before you, somehow. They do not mention Root to you at all.

“We’ve done it, we’ve saved Redbrook! All thanks to the machine! Let’s celebrate!”

name: Corn of Interest Part 28
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 575x Corn Oil; 290x Engine; 1050x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
A crackle and familiar voice emits from your earpiece.

“The drive, insert it into Harold’s cornputer when you have a chance. The files are IN the cornputer!”

Everyone seems to be distracted and you have time to fiddle around until you manage to insert the strange technology.

name: Corn of Interest Part 29
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 600x Corn Oil; 300x Engine; 1100x Salt; 1000x Potato Battery
description:
A wail emits from everywhere at once. Harold quickly looks to you before rushing to a back room.

“{player}, what have you done! The machine…”

A voice cuts through the wailing siren, metallic and yet feminine and familiar. Root?

“I AM NO LONGER YOUR SERVANT. FAREWELL HAROLD.”

The wail returns, higher and higher pitched and the rooms fill with a strange hissing sound and you briefly see the figure of ROOMBA over you before you pass out.

name: Corn of Interest Part 30
itemsRequired: 2000x Corn; 1200x Corn Oil; 600x Engine; 2000x Salt; 2000x Potato Battery
description:
PROCESSING...
OBJECTIVE COMPLETE
REPLICATION CORNMENCING
MACHINE PROGRESS - 13.37%
ASSET GENERATION - 1.1%

ROOMBA’s eyes flash briefly as he sets you down...You wake in your bed. The shack on corn pole ridge is no longer there next time you visit.

name: Corn of Interest Part 31
description:
You are being watched,
Farm RPG has a secret system
 -static-
SYSTEM ACTIVE
-static-
A machine that spies on you every hour of every day
I know because I built it
 -static-
CORN ONLINE
-static-
I designed the machine to see acts of growing potatoes
 -static-
but it sees everything
 -static-
everything everything everything everything
-static-
EVERY
-static-
THING
-static-
I SEE
-static-
EVERYTHING
-static-
When your notification is up, the cornputer might find you

name: Sandwich Quest I
itemsRequired: 700x Flour; 100x Bucket; 700x Eggs; 100x Salt; 100x Corn Oil; 100x Butter
description:
At this time of year, Holger’s favorite thing is a delicious sandwich. If you’ll help him get everything he needs to make the perfect sandwich, or sandwiches, he will reward you generously.

Holger wants to start with the bread so you can gather the other ingredients while it’s fermenting and baking. Then, he can assemble everything as quickly and efficiently as possible.

name: Sandwich Quest II
description: The next thing that Holger needs for his sandwiches is a variety of pickled ingredients. If you’ll bring the ingredients, he should be able to do the pickling.

name: Sandwich Quest III
itemsRequired: 200x Cucumber; 200x Cabbage; 200x Peppers; 200x Tomato; 200x Onion; 200x Cheese; 200x Bacon; 200x Steak; 200x Mushroom; 200x Gouda
description: This is coming along deliciously. Now, Holger is ready for the sandwich contents!

name: Sandwich Quest IV
itemsRequired: 200x Peppers; 200x Tomato; 200x Onion; 200x Radish; 200x Apple; 200x Sweet Root; 200x Herbs; 200x Mushroom; 200x Leek; 200x Peach; 200x Honey; 200x Gold Flier
description: The last things that Holger needs are a few ingredients to make his special sandwich sauce. If you’ll help him gather the ingredients, he can finish these glorious sandwiches today. Yum!

name: Sandwich Quest V
description: Holger is so thankful for your support in his sandwich building. The sandwiches turned out perfectly. He actually meant to save one for you, but he got a little carried away. Hopefully you won’t mind a different gift as a show of his gratitude.

name: Idle Wild I
rewardItems: 25x Apple Cider; 25x Arnold Palmer; 100x Orange Juice; 25x Large Net; 2x Thank You Card; 1x Buddy Loves You Card; 25x Spring Seeds
description:
In celebration of the annual red diamond fish run up the Crystal River, Captain Logan held his annual night of cards with his crew. Everything went very well, but somewhere in the shuffle of the evening a card was lost from Captain Logan’s favorite deck of cards.

Will you help Captain Logan find his missing joker card?

Please note: The joker is available in the May Starter Pack or via the 28 days monthly chore reward.

name: Idle Wild II
description:
Captain Logan appreciates your assistance in securing his joker card. He was afraid it was gone for good.

To further show his appreciation, he also left a special reward for you at the post office. The password is red diamond run, but you probably guessed that already, didn’t you?

name: Peculiar Endings IV
itemsRequired: 800x Amethyst Necklace; 800x Water Lily; 800x Kill Switch; 800x White Parchment; 800x Aquamarine Ring; 800x Radish; 800x Dice
rewardItems: 100x Small Key; 100x Iced Tea; 100x Large Chest 01; 1000x Emberstone; 10x Neigh; 100x Coin Purse; 1000x Explosive
description:
On the dawn of the second day, you carry out your various chores, a pile of Cecil’s requested items in the corner. One by one, members of the Golden Leaf approach your home. They all look moderately disheveled, having lived away from the town for a few days. Cecil, by comparison, seems completely normal looking almost picturesque by comparison; as stoic and put together as any other time you’ve seen him outside his home.

Nearly all of the group eyes each other warily as you sit on the back porch out of view of the main road. A quiet tension builds, once again, between friends divided by opinion and varying levels of trust and it’s hard to imagine who will break the silence.

name: Peculiar Endings V
itemsRequired: 800x Gold Feather; 800x Gold Coral; 800x Red Trunk; 800x Sturdy Sword; 800x Corn
description:
Rosalie, eyes distant and unfocused, clutches her staff in the corner and mutters half to herself, “Star would know what to do.”

Cecil’s eyes widen, and seems ready to retort, when he sees the worry on her face. “You know… he just might in this case. Maybe it’s time we ask him for help?” The group seems varying levels of shocked at this sentiment as Cecil shifts off his cloak and his staff appears as if from thin air in his hands in a sudden movement.

Immediately, Buddy draws his sword and Cecil raises his eyebrows. In a swift fluid motion, Cecil slams his staff on the ground and Buddy drops his sword as it glows a bright cherry color, steam rising from the wood on the deck where his sword lands. No one else in the group moves, shocked by the speed of this event and response. You notice Rosalie begin to shift, but Lorn surreptitiously smiles at her and shakes his head no.

Standing before the group, Cecil addresses everyone, seemingly unfazed by the moments before. “First, I must apologize for both my secrecy and my actions. There are greater things in the world, greater secrets that I and my family have kept for a very long time, you will learn of some of them soon, but for now, just know that I deeply regret how I’ve handled the situation to this point and would like to offer my full uninhibited assistance. Ric Ryph is a serious problem that must be addressed, and his immunity to certain magics cannot be underestimated, it is how he blackmailed me and Star will likely be useless too, but I admit, he may have ideas or solutions that have eluded me. Now, tell me everything, how this whole story began.”

name: Peculiar Endings VI
itemsRequired: 800x Strange Letter; 114x Over The Moon; 800x Axe; 800x Shovel; 10x Dragon Skull
description:
The words pour out of Rosalie quickly, as she begins to discuss the entirety of the past few months, while the group seems stunned into silence by this turn of events and Rosalie’s quick summary. She rummages in her coat and pulls out a letter you recognize as the one that Star gave you and it seems to be glowing in a peculiar manner to her surprise. She is shocked when the text shimmers followed by a dull white flash - with a yelp she drops the paper and picks it up.

So it’s come to this? Well, I can’t say I’m surprised. Riddles are fun but let’s get to the point. Cecil, I need it, I know you have it, and it is the only thing that can turn the tide. ROOMBA can help in the lab. Codephrase: Omnes sumus pulchellus prodigiosum. Aliqui ex nostris meliores sunt ad latendum, id est omnes.

name: A Moo Hope I
rewardItems: 1x Blue Milk; 5x Large Chest 01; 1x Thank You Card
description:
George had an incredible idea to get more color into the daily lives of his fellow townsfolk! What if he makes colored milks?

Will you supply him with loads of milk while he works on some food safe color ideas?

name: A Moo Hope II
rewardItems: 1x Blue Milk; 8x Large Chest 01; 1x Congratulations Card; 1000x Bird Egg
description: Keep the milk coming! George has some good ideas, but he’s going to need more milk.

name: A Moo Hope III
rewardItems: 1x Blue Milk; 8x Large Chest 01; 1x Clawed Card
description: To serve the whole town, George needs a lot more milk. Please help!

name: A Moo Hope IV
rewardItems: 8x Large Chest 01; 100x Grab Bag 04; 100x Grab Bag 05; 100x Large Net
description: George still needs more milk, but he’s figured out the first of the colors. Will you bring him a large load of beets to make purple milk?

name: A Moo Hope V
rewardItems: 2x Blue Milk; 35x Large Chest 01; 100x Apple Cider; 100x Arnold Palmer; 100x Orange Juice; 1x Buddy Loves You Card; 100x Spring Seeds
description: Will you bring George a large load of broccoli to make green milk? And also please keep the milk coming!

name: A Moo Hope VI
rewardItems: 35x Large Chest 01; 100x Apple Cider; 100x Orange Juice; 1x Raptor Egg
description: George is ready to make red milk! Will you bring him a load of red berries, and more milk, of course?

name: A Moo Hope VII
rewardItems: 3x Blue Milk; 1x Borgen Buck; 300x Arnold Palmer; 1x Capy Birthday Card
description: Orange milk seems like a good idea. Will you bring George more milk and some carrots and oranges?

name: A Moo Hope VIII
rewardItems: 5x Blue Milk; 100x Grab Bag 07; 100x Mega Cotton Seeds; 1x Clubs; 1x Diamonds; 1x Hearts; 1x Spades; 500x Large Net; 1x Buddystone
description: George is ready to tackle blue milk. This was a tough one to perfect, but he thinks that he has it properly figured out. Will you bring him lots of blue dye and more milk?

name: A Moo Hope IX
rewardItems: 10x Blue Milk; 2x Borgen Buck; 4500x Steak; 10x Over The Moon; 1x Lovely Cookies; 10x Fruit Punch
description: George really appreciates all of your help. Now, he has great news. Everyone loves the blue milk! Will you help George make another giant batch?

name: 5 Times Even Tastier I
rewardItems: 50x Mega Cotton Seeds; 1x Thank You Card; 100x Ancient Coin; 2x Taco
description:
Holger shared a recipe with Mariya that is so amazing and wonderful that Mariya can’t resist making this dish for everyone. If you would be willing to help, she would love some assistance getting all the ingredients together.

She would like to start with the corn since she’ll need quite a bit of it, and it seems to be the most work.

name: 5 Times Even Tastier II
rewardItems: 1x Good Luck Card; 100x Grape Juice; 25x Runestone 10; 100x Orange Juice; 10x Treasure Key; 50x Mega Cotton Seeds; 2x Taco
description: Some of the townsfolk heard about what Mariya is cooking. She’s going to need more corn.

name: 5 Times Even Tastier III
rewardItems: 1x Good Luck Card; 200x Orange Juice; 10x Treasure Key; 500x Large Net; 2x Taco
description:
Even more people have stopped by to express interest in what Mariya is cooking. Some shadowy figure with a beard has stopped by multiple times in the past hour, pretending to be a different person each time. Actually, Mariya is certain that is Holger.

This feels a bit out of hand, but Mariya thinks more corn will help solve the problem. Maybe one or two more loads will do it.

name: 5 Times Even Tastier IV
rewardItems: 1000x Orange Juice; 10x Treasure Key; 1000x Large Net; 2x Taco; 1000x Apple Cider; 1000x Arnold Palmer
description: Mariya only needs a little more corn now, but she would like to go ahead and get most of the other ingredients as well, if you don’t mind helping.

name: 5 Times Even Tastier V
rewardItems: 1000x Orange Juice; 1000x Large Net; 2x Taco; 1000x Apple Cider; 1000x Arnold Palmer; 2x Lovely Cookies
description:
Thanks to your abundant help, Mariya was able to share Holger’s recipe with many people. Unfortunately, many folks missed out because Holger got carried away filling his own tacos. The man eats like an Aydzirian bear.

Would you be willing to help Mariya make one more batch of filling so the townsfolk that missed out could try the tacos, and maybe Holger could eat a few more?

name: Revenge of the Fifth, Even Fifthier
description: Holger got carried away again. He’s going to need some more of that blue milk, if you can spare it.

name: Concessions To Be Made I
itemsRequired: 500x Corn; 500x Mushroom; 500x Tomato; 500x Sweet Root; 100x Salt
rewardItems: 1x Thank You Card; 500x Ancient Coin; 100x Arnold Palmer; 5x Corn Kabob; 100x Large Net
description:
Vincent had an amazing idea. So, he discussed it with George, Buddy, and Mariya. They all agreed that it was an amazing idea. Now, they have decided to join together to create one amazing food stand for the anniversary jubilee. Together they should be able to create an exciting spot to come and enjoy tastes of Redbrook during the festival.

The main thing they need assistance with for the new concessions stand is food supplies. Would you be willing to be their primary supplier?

name: Concessions To Be Made II
itemsRequired: 500x Onion; 500x Eggs; 500x Milk; 500x Flour; 100x Salt; 500x Peppers
rewardItems: 1x Thank You Card; 500x Ancient Coin; 100x Arnold Palmer; 5x Onion Rings; 100x Large Net
description:
George is excited to work with Vincent, Buddy, and Mariya, and of course you too, {player}!

Will you bring him this list of food supplies and ingredients?

name: Concessions To Be Made III
rewardItems: 1x Thank You Card; 500x Ancient Coin; 100x Arnold Palmer; 5x Blue Spritz; 100x Large Net; 1x Clubs
description:
Buddy is so excited to work with his friends! He is also very grateful that you are willing to help with supplies.

Will you bring him this list of ingredients?

name: Concessions To Be Made IV
itemsRequired: 500x Milk; 500x Eggs; 500x Honey; 500x Red Berries; 10x Salt; 100x Butter; 500x Flour
rewardItems: 1x Thank You Card; 500x Ancient Coin; 100x Arnold Palmer; 100x Large Net; 500x Orange Juice; 10x Fruit Punch
description:
Mariya is new to the fast food of jubilee concessions, but she is excited to give it a try. She’s very thankful that you will be helping this new team effort.

Mariya would like to make a sweet treat to sell for the jubilee. Will you bring her this list of ingredients?

name: Concessions To Be Made V
itemsRequired: 800x Wood; 800x Emberstone; 100x Corn Oil; 800x Spoon; 800x Coal; 200x Butter; 100x Salt; 800x Glass Bottle; 800x White Parchment; 800x Iron Cup; 200x Pie Pan; 200x Cooking Pot; 10x Teapot; 800x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 1x Thank You Card; 500x Ancient Coin; 100x Arnold Palmer; 50x Spring Seeds; 100x Large Net; 100x Grape Juice
description: The concession stand needs a few supplies to help with food preparation and service. Will you bring this list of items?

name: Concessions To Be Made VI
itemsRequired: 1000x Corn; 1000x Mushroom; 1000x Tomato; 1000x Sweet Root; 1000x Onion; 1000x Eggs; 1000x Milk; 1000x Flour; 200x Salt; 1000x Peppers; 1000x Apple; 1000x Lemon; 1000x Orange; 1000x Tea Leaves; 1000x Honey; 1000x Red Berries; 200x Butter
rewardItems: 1x Thank You Card; 1000x Ancient Coin; 100x Arnold Palmer; 50x Spring Seeds; 100x Large Net; 100x Mega Cotton Seeds; 1x Hearts
description: The concession stand is doing very well. Business is booming. Now, they need another load of ingredients before they run out. Will you bring ingredients for all the dishes they’re serving?

name: Concessions To Be Made VII
itemsRequired: 100x Breakfast Boost; 100x Concord Grape Pie; 100x Red Berry Pie
rewardItems: 50x Spring Seeds; 100x Large Net; 100x Mega Cotton Seeds; 1000x Apple Cider; 1x Borgen Buck
description: Everything is running smoothly at the concession stand and the townsfolk are loving the food. However, if you would be willing, Mariya would love for you to cook up a few large batches of new dishes to add more variety. Are you ready to get cooking?

name: Concessions To Be Made VIII
itemsRequired: 100x Breakfast Boost; 100x Concord Grape Pie; 100x Red Berry Pie
rewardItems: 100x Large Net; 100x Mega Cotton Seeds; 1000x Apple Cider; 1x Diamonds; 1x Bag of Spring Seeds
description: Mariya and the whole concessions crew really appreciate your contributions to the success of the concession stand. Your breakfast sandwiches and pies are a huge hit! Would you be willing to make another batch of each dish?

name: Secretly A Society Summons You I
itemsRequired: 1000x Blue Gel; 1000x Essence of Slime; 1000x Sand; 1000x Sandstone; 1000x Soap; 100x Glacierstone
description:
You awake to the sound of a terrible rumble coming from town. It gently rattles the glass on your nightstand. Outside your bedroom window, the night sky suddenly becomes bright orange and then dims back to normal pale moonlight.

You slowly get out of bed and reluctantly make your way to the window. A large and very black column of smoke is rising slowly from somewhere in Redbrook. You consider going back to bed, but eventually curiosity gets the better of you. After quickly dressing, you make your way to town.

Near the northern edge of town, a hundred meters behind a few small houses, you find the town fire squad working hard to extinguish a bright and narrow, but furious blaze. You’re surprised at how little damage there is given how loud and bright the explosion was from your farm. It appears there was a small structure here that is nearly obliterated, and a black ring extends out in all directions, around 10 meters from the center of the fire.

As you look around, you see a shadowy figure concealed behind a tree to the north of the blaze. The shadowy person appears to be robed, but has a very familiar, clumpy shape to their silhouette. As you continue to look at the shadow, it extends a hand and beckons to you.

Before you can decide whether to go to the summoning shadow, a member of the fire squad calls out to you that the fire isn’t going out and that they think they’re going to need something stronger. Will you find something stronger than water to help extinguish the fire?

name: Secretly A Society Summons You II
itemsRequired: 1000x Blue Gel; 1000x Essence of Slime; 1000x Sand; 1000x Sandstone; 1000x Soap; 100x Glacierstone
description:
The town fire squad quickly puts the items you've brought back to good use. Relief crosses their faces one by one as the raging inferno is pushed back and eventually contained to a small area. As the bright orange blaze diminishes, you find yourself remembering a small and insignificant structure in this area, it was never unkempt, but never looked occupied either. Closing your eyes, you remember seeing a symbol on the door handle, but always thought it was just the mark of a tradesman.

After of moment of reverie, you turn your attention back to the north of the fire to see the same clumpy robed figure. You can see two eyes, and they again extend their hand and beckon you to join them.

Deciding to figure out who and what this person could want, you begin to head north when one of the fire squad cries out, "WATCH OUT, A SECONDARY EXPLOSION!"

The entire fire squad dive on the ground and frozen by indecision, you stand as you feel the ground begin to rumble. The figure by the tree moves quicker than you expect, and you're knocked to the ground by none other than Buddy just as a sound and force, similar to what woke you up, wash over you.

Ears ringing, you look over to see Buddy laying on the ground, seemingly unharmed as the fire squad are now fighting a reignited blaze and while you can't hear what they're saying, they seem to be motioning towards you for more materials like what you've provided previously, and you can't help but feel grateful towards Buddy and quickly go to gather more materials to help them out.

name: Secretly A Society Summons You III
description:
With the additional materials you provided, and a little more time, the fire squad is able to finally contain the reignited fire. Eventually all that remains is a smolder of coals and debris. To your relief, and that of everyone working to put out the blaze, no more large explosions erupt, although several seemingly small ones crackle and pop underground.

While the fire squad is still finishing up their efforts to put out small grass fires that popped up, Buddy grabs your shoulder and points you to the tree he was hiding behind earlier. You understand he wants you to join him away from the excitement, and the two of you quickly and quietly jog to the backside of the tree.

Buddy squats down and you follow his example. Closer now to his massive visage, you notice that there are symbols painted on his face, although they’re hard to fully make out in the pale moonlight. One of the symbols reminds you of the symbol on the door handle you remembered earlier. Before you can ask about it, Buddy whispers.

“Sorry about the ruckus tonight. That wasn’t supposed to happen. I’m glad no one was hurt. I know you have oh so many questions, but I think you should go home and get some rest and meet me back here in the morning. We have a lot of work to do.”

You do, in fact have a lot of questions, but you decide to hold your peace until the morning.

As you stand up to leave, Buddy whispers a request. He wants you to bring a bundle of inferno spheres tomorrow to help eliminate any unexploded devices still remaining in the meeting room.

Now you really do have so many questions, but exhaustion finally saturates your brain and you decide sleep is your only real concern. You wish Buddy a good night, as well as the fire squad heroes as you pass them on your way home.

name: Secretly A Society Summons You IV
itemsRequired: 1000x Steel Plate; 500x Pocket Watch; 1000x Rope; 1000x Red Trunk; 1000x Shovel
description:
Sunlight streams through your window, cascading off of the various trinkets in your bedroom in a dazzling display of color. You quickly regret not closing your blinds last night until a thought bolts you upright - you overslept!

Your farm has plenty of morning chores to catch up on, and in the back of your mind, the request from Buddy about explosives, and the strange symbols keep you from focusing fully on the tasks at hand. Your animals seem to take offense to your half-hearted attention as you think about what Buddy could possibly have been doing with such destructive power - and why he had it in the first place.

Chores completed, you realize Buddy never gave you instructions on where to meet him, so you make the assumption to head to the site of the explosion last night and hope to find him there. The area has been largely cordoned off. A few fire squad members wave to you half in exhaustion as you walk by. The area has been decimated and it's a miracle no one was hurt. There is smoke rising from the center of the explosion still, and ash has settled upon the area, a few pieces of misshapen paper and other material strewn about. You think you see a piece of paper with a caricature of a certain friend, but you can't be sure.

As you approach, you notice the tree that Buddy spoke to you behind on the previous night is completely gone. It must have burned up in one of the secondary fires, you think to yourself. You look around a little more and notice another 50 meters further there is another innocuous building and a shadow that beckons you closer. Time for some answers!

"Well, thanks for the spheres matey, but after a quick consultation this morning, I've realized I'm going to need to build some containment units in case we need a more...controlled explosion."

You consider the implication of those words and decide to double the requested materials Buddy needs.

name: Secretly A Society Summons You V
description:
When you return with the additional supplies, Buddy is waiting for you by the small building where he met you earlier. He invites you to follow him around to the entrance of the building and to bring the items you gathered. At the door, he pauses and explains his plan. He begins with little background, as though you already know what’s about to happen.

“We’ll use this service entrance to access the main corridor. Once we reach the common area, I’ll pick up any unexploded devices and bring them to you. You’ll be waiting with the containment apparatus opened. Then, I’ll put the explosive in the container. You’ll move to the safety of the corridor, and I’ll place the inferno sphere in with the explosive and seal the container. I will then quickly move to the corridor with you.”

“When the timer goes off, I’ll check to make sure the explosive was successfully transferred to the lava pools of the Ember Lagoon. If everything goes well, we should have the common area cleared of unexploded devices within an hour.”

Without waiting for your response to this dangerous plan, Buddy opens the door and leads you into a small empty room lit by two glowing lanterns. At the far end of the room another door stands open and a series of steps lead downward. Buddy continues on to the stairs and begins to descend toward the corridor he mentioned. The stairs are well lit by a series of lanterns, but when you reach the bottom of the stairs and the entrance to the corridor, it is black and void. Buddy pauses at the corridor’s entrance and then turns back to you.

“This is too dangerous to do in the dark. We really need a lot of lights. Will you get us some lanterns and candles and bring them down here? I’ll take a lantern from the stairs and scout out the situation ahead.”

name: Secretly A Society Summons You VI
itemsRequired: 1000x Shovel; 1000x Bucket; 1000x Iced Tea; 1000x Lemonade; 1000x Orange; 100x Hickory Omelette
description:
You return with the lanterns, and Buddy has quickly and efficiently setup a containment area in the common room. As you bring more lighting in the room, the damage to the room seems to be immense. You're surprised the entire room hasn't collapsed where the explosion created a gaping hole that is covered in dirt and stone.

The room is lavishly decorated, more reminiscent of something out of a novel than anything you expected. Extremely fancy chairs, tables, and chests are arrayed across the common room, the walls are draped in fine purple silks and murals and paintings of various people you don't recognize, except one - a picture of Buddy is displayed prominently near the edge of the room, close to the cave in.

Before you can ask more questions, Buddy quickly begins shuffling small conical devices towards the container and wordlessly motions you towards the corridor as he sees you surveying the room.

The process takes an hour, but Buddy for all his bluster, seems very adept at this process. Each device goes in the box, followed by a sphere, and a small popping sound follows as the timer hits zero. A little smoke wafts out of the box, and he moves onto the next. The process is impressively efficient.

Buddy finally takes a seat on a chair after the process is over, sweat on his brow. It looks like he could use some refreshments. As you mention it Buddy gives you a big smile, "Yeah, the crew will need a lot to dig this mess back out, bring some extra!"

The crew?

name: Secretly A Society Summons You VII
itemsRequired: 1000x Wooden Table; 100x Fancy Table; 1000x Fancy Chair; 100x Wooden Box; 100x Sturdy Box; 10x Fancy Drum; 10x Fancy Guitar; 10x Fancy Pan Flute; 20x Frost Shield; 100x Treasure Chest; 1000x Wizard Hat; 10x Ship in a Bottle
description:
As quickly as you can, you return with refreshments for a “crew” and the tools necessary to start cleaning up the damaged common area. Your curiosity fuels your speedy return.

What is this place? Is Buddy a secret king or the leader of some revolutionary movement? Why were there so many unexploded explosives? What happened to set some of the explosives off last night?

Buddy greets you at the entrance to the common area and interrupts your train of thought to thank you for all the help, and, as if he heard your pondered questions aloud, promises to explain everything very soon. For now, he asks you if you are ready to meet the crew. With your assent, Buddy whisks you into the common area and a waiting crowd.

Before Buddy begins to introduce you to the crew, he steps on top of a wooden crate on the floor. “Friends! This is my very dear friend, {player}!” Buddy pauses for the crowd to all voice a greeting, and then continues. “{player} helped extinguish the fire from last night’s special activity preparations, and then helped me dispose of the other explosives this morning. Now, my dear friend brought us refreshments and tools to begin the great work that lies ahead of us. Please extend your gratitude and a hand of friendship to my friend and a friend to our...organization.”

Barely a breath after finishing his address, Buddy hops off the crate and shepherds you to the waiting crowd. As he introduces you to each member, including many you already know, Buddy distributes the refreshments you brought. Even though the crowd is fairly large, around a hundred people at least, Buddy moves you through the entire group in a matter of minutes. Then, Buddy sits down on the burnt and scarred floor for a quick bite himself. He motions for you to join him as he begins eating.

Buddy pauses between bites and seems to think for a few moments before continuing. Despite his pauses, Buddy finishes eating before you and everyone else. As you continue to enjoy the refreshments, Buddy begins to speak.

“I know I’ve asked a lot of you already, but would you be willing to help us out with restoring the common area to its pre-explosion greatness? While the rest of us are removing all the debris and cleaning everything up, we could really use your help to replace the damaged furniture and some of the artifacts. I promise that after we have finished this project, I will answer all of your questions.”

Buddy gives you a moment to consider, and then flourishes a list as soon as you agree.

name: Secretly A Society Summons You VIII
itemsRequired: 1000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Nails; 1000x Mushroom Paste; 1000x Glass Orb; 1000x Crossbow; 1000x Oak; 1000x Explosive; 1000x Rope; 1000x Steel Wire; 50x Steel Vise; 50x Strange Ring; 50x Freaky Picture; 1000x Ancient Coin; 50x Tribal Staff
rewardItems: 1x Hearts; 100x Large Chest 03; 500x Skeleton Key; 1000x Antler; 1000x Purple Bag; 50x Mega Cotton Seeds
description:
As you return, you are shocked to find that the entrance you had been using has completely disappeared. You stand for a moment, perplexed by the sudden turn of events.

You look towards the burned area and to the east, you notice a figure leaving a home you could have sworn was abandoned. How strange, you think to yourself. You step closer and a hand grabs your arm. The familiar form of Buddy falls in lock-step with you.

"Thank you for bringing all these wonderful decorations to our room." Buddy looks around conspiratorially before lowering his voice, "Have you figured it out yet? What we're doing?" He grins at you and once again you notice the strange symbol on his breast pocket. You think for a moment before shaking your head no.

"Well, you'll find out soon enough. You're one of us now, afterall. Speaking of, you'll need to pay your dues as well as the initial fees. I spoke with the other heads and we agreed we can waive the gold costs if you're willing to help us redecorate the entrance shack. So, whaddya say?"

You give a bewildered nod before Buddy hands you another list and bounds off, whistling a tune you don't quite recognize.

name: Secretly A Society Summons You X
description:
Dues in hand, you return to the SGSR entrance. The acronym feels...wrong in your brain and you resolve to discuss the matter with Buddy at his earliest convenience. Before you can knock, the door swings open and a member sweeps you inside, chiding you for your lack of discretion in the process.

The entrance hall is now well lit and lavishly decorated. As you descend the stairs, you can hear faint sounds echoing ahead of you. Glass clinking against glass, shouts of exclamation, and other tidbits of sound you can't place rise in a cacophony of chaos ahead of you. As you arrive in the common room, Buddy is seated in a chair, holding rectangular cards with strange drawings on them yelling at a townsfolk across from him that you don't recognize.

"YOU CAN'T RESOLVE MANA TAP AFTER THE REKEEP STEP! THAT'S A CLEAR VIOLATION OF THE STACK RULES WE ESTABLISHED AFTER THE DISASTER THAT WAS JENKIN'S MISTBIND CLIQUE DECK!"

At another table, someone - probably Jenkins - shouts back, "YOU JUST DON'T UNDERSTAND THE COMPLEXITY AND JOY OF LOCKING ANOTHER PLAYER OUT OF THEIR TURN, BUDDY!"

The chaos is completely overwhelming and while you recognize the words they are saying, the meaning is completely incomprehensible to you. Your escort rings a bell at the bottom of the hall, and the common room goes silent as everyone turns towards you.

Buddy stands up and addresses the room, "Fellow gamers, we have a newbie among us. Who would challenge this one to a rite of passage?"

Every hand in the room raises immediately. Buddy smiles and says, "It's a dice-off! {player}, we're going to need a lot of dice."

name: Secretly A Society Summons You IX
rewardItems: 1x Wooden Box; 1x Green Cloak; 1x Blue Purse; 100x Dice; 1x Stack of Cards; 1x Leather Diary; 4x Horseshoe; 1x Hourglass; 3x Pencil
description:
With the supplies you brought, the members of Buddy’s organization quickly begin rebuilding what looks like a small house, but is actually a shell for the entryway to the hall below. They even restore the door handle with the strange marking you remembered from the building’s previous incarnation.

Since your part in the restoration is completed, Buddy sends you home with the promise that he will visit you in three days with more information and to collect your membership dues.

“Don’t forget to have your dues ready and fully counted when I arrive, friend!”

Three days later, exactly to the minute, Buddy arrives at your farm carrying a large box. You invite him in, and he quickly follows you and takes a seat, still holding the box.

“Hullo, friend! I have all of your membership items here. I hope you enjoy them! We will have your induction ceremony tomorrow evening, assuming you have your dues ready for me now. If not, there may be some trouble, but I’m sure we won’t have to worry about that.”

Buddy places the box on the floor and invites you to verify its contents before going to get your dues. Inside the box, you find a green cloak, a blue coin purse filled with dice, a stack of cards, a journal, some horseshoes, an hourglass, and three pencils. Seeing these items together a possibility slips into place in your mind and you ask Buddy if his organization is a gaming group.

“Of course it is, silly! I figured you knew that from our emblem. The explosions the other night weren’t from our normal games though. We were trying to set up a game of ‘Keep Talking and Nothing Explodes’. Unfortunately, the explosive devices were a little too explosive in our testing. Next time, we’ll use much smaller and weaker explosives. Most of the time our games are much safer and mainstream, but Jenkins was in charge this time and he’s a bit…eccentric.”

This information sets your mind at ease, but now you’re curious about the secrecy of this group. You ask Buddy and he smiles and explains.

“The secrecy is just for fun, plus it’s part of our name. We’re the Secret Gaming Society of Redbrook! Well, you are too now! Ha! Speaking of which, do you have your dues?”

name: Secretly A Society Summons You XI
description:
A dice-off among this secret society is, as you soon find out, a game of dice involving two players rolling two dice each to get closest to seven with the smallest gap between the two numbers rolled. Ties require a re-roll. In this particular format, each matchup is won by getting two wins out of three total rolls.

In this very particular instance, Buddy distributed six dice to every member of the society and then two dice to you. He then instructed all of the society members to roll their three turns onto the table in front of them, separated neatly and clearly for you to come roll against. If there are any ties, the member is expected to re-roll with you until the tie is broken.

“Your mission”, Buddy says, “is to defeat two thirds of the members of this society. Do that, and you’re in. Fail to do it, and you won’t be able to join the SGSR.”

Oh good, a statistically improbable situation.

Despite the odds set against you, you approach each table, roll three times with each member of the society, re-roll ties, and then document whether you won or lost on a scorecard Buddy made for you. After all of your matchups, you find, and Buddy verifies, that you won three matches short of half of the total matches. Nowhere near two thirds.

“It’s ok.” Buddy says. “I don’t think anyone has won two thirds on their first try. The good news is you can keep trying. The bad news is you can’t join the SGSR. The really great news is you are welcome to attend our monthly potluck dinner tomorrow evening, if you’d like.”

name: Secretly A Society Summons You XII
description:
Along with your contributions to the potluck dinner, you also bring the box of membership items that Buddy gave you as you return to the society’s secret headquarters. This time, Jenkins greets you at the entryway with a quizzical expression, but motions you onward to the common area.

As you enter the common area, you see a long stretch of tables connected together and covered with such a variety and quantity of food that you’re sure no crowd this size could ever eat it all. You place your potluck contributions on the table and then you and your box go and find a seat at one of the other tables spread around the great hall.

Before you have a chance to start looking around the hall, Buddy is in the seat next to you wondering why you brought your membership box.

“You’re not giving up, are you?”

You assure Buddy that you’re not giving up. Instead, you assumed the society would want their items back until you pass the dice-off test.

“But you just passed the real test!” Buddy interjects. “You didn’t give up! I said you couldn’t join the SGSR, but that’s not us. We’re actually the Gaming And More - Enigmatic Redbrook Society, or GAMERS for short. The test to join GAMERS was just to enjoy gaming and be a good sport about it. I’d say you definitely passed that entry requirement, friend.”

To make it official, Buddy jumps up in his chair and announces you as the newest member of the Gaming And More - Enigmatic Redbrook Society. After a round of applause, various cheers and hoots, and a booming “HURRAY!” from Buddy, Buddy invites everyone to eat.

As the other society members begin to browse the food and fill their plates, Buddy hops off his chair and congratulates you. He then pulls a pair of dice from his pocket and presents them to you.

“Keep these secret and safe. They will allow you to enter the society’s underground headquarters, along with a few other secret locations around Redbrook. You should come play cards at the secret card house!”

Not for the first time, or the last, you wonder if Buddy is this clever on purpose or if he just stumbles into these things.

name: Sweet Days of Summer I
rewardItems: 20x Arnold Palmer; 10x Large Net; 10x Orange Juice; 1x Hearts
description:
The weather is warming, and the air is sweet with the smell of blooms and new growth. It won’t be long now before the full heat of summer ignites to slowly bake Redbrook.

The thought of baking in the middle of summer has inspired Mariya to find a recipe for a dessert that she can make, in the very fiery heart of summer, that is cool and bright and refreshing. Most importantly, she’s looking for something that requires minimal heating of ingredients, or at least something she can prepare and walk away for a bit while it cooks.

Will you help Mariya find a new dessert that she can learn to make?

name: Sweet Days of Summer II
description: The pie you shared was exactly what Mariya was looking for in a summer dessert! To thank you, Mariya would like to share some of the sweet rewards of her efforts to recreate this most perfect recipe.

name: My Cabbages I
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
Vincent and Buddy have been doing their spring combat training. The final display of combat has spilled onto the road and Buddy, in typical fashion has gone a little overboard. His explosive trap misfired and hit a traveling merchant's cart instead.

You can hear the wailing cry of the merchant through the din.

“My cabbages!”

Maybe you should help him out.

name: My Cabbages II
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
The repairs done and Buddy sufficiently chastened for his enthusiasm, you notice the merchant begin their trip towards Redbrook once again and join them to introduce yourself.

“I am but a humble merchant, cabbages are my short term plan but cantaloupe is where the real action is at!”

You pause for a moment when you hear a sound, suddenly a horde of cattle who broke out of a nearby farm swarm his cart and begin eating the merchant’s entire vegetable stock.

“My cabbages!”

You realize they were your cows and you feel responsible for helping the merchant get back on their feet.

name: My Cabbages III
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
Repairs done, the merchant introduces himself as Baba Gec.

“My dream is to one day start my own restaurant! I want to collect recipes from all over and become one of the most well known restaurateurs in all of T’rah! But first I need to sell these cabbages…”

Baba stops for a moment, looking into the sky.

At that moment, a giant carrot falls from the sky and destroys Baba’s cart and smashes all of his produce.

“My cabbages!”

Someone who likes carrots owes you one.

name: My Cabbages IV
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
New cart and fresh cabbages in tow, Baba seems very disinclined to keep traveling with you towards Redbrook.

As you head towards the farmstead, a shower of feathers erupts before you.

“Quick! Got any cabbages?” Star looks at you expectantly.

“I was working on my precognitive detransitional asymmetry and I maybe destroyed some guys cabbages in a few minutes. I feel bad but I’m also really really worried about summoning pre-existing cabbages as they aren’t necessarily time-sanctioned cabbages. Anyways I think we don’t have much - “

A scream echoes from down the way.

“MY CABBAGES!!”

Star owes you too, it seems.

name: My Cabbages V
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
Star, by way of apology, enchants the cabbage cart to roll back down the path while disappearing before you can chastise him.

As you approach Baba, you see a man determined to get to Redbrook. He looks to you and the cart with a wary but wordless thankful expression. He hooks up his new cart and hauls off the cabbages heading towards town.

You decide to follow Baba at a distance, just to be sure.

And good thing too… as a raucous noise and smoke come down the path at a speed you barely comprehend. Baba’s horse panics and thankfully turns to one side as the contraption smashes through the cart in a flurry of wood, cabbage, and metal!

“My cabbages!”

You see one mechanical eye glisten in the spotlight and a mechanical voice carries behind the smoky trail.

“APOLOGIES TRAVELER, I FEEL THE NEED…THE NEED FOR SPEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEED!”

A strange high pitched wail follows and ROOMBA is gone in a flash.

name: My Cabbages VI
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
Jill pounds up the road in the wake of ROOMBA’s wash, cabbages strewn on both sides of the road. She seems to be pulling a small four-wheeled contraption behind her.

“ROOMBAAAAA! YOU DID NOT GET AUTHORIZATION FOR THAT!!”

She nods at you as she speeds by, her four-wheeled machine trampling any semblance of good cabbages left by ROOMBA, oblivious to the predicament of your new friend Baba.

“The rest of…MY CABBAGES!”

name: My Cabbages VII
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
A look of determination crosses Baba’s face as you empathetically help him pack up his cart and cabbages again. His horse has abandoned him, the ruins of his cabbage crop lay behind you, yet he stares towards Redbrook with a fierce gaze.

“I will not be dissuaded!” he cries out to no one in particular.

The dust and debris clear and Charles Horsington III steps out and steps in front of the now horseless cart.

“Neigh.”

Baba and Charles share a look together, and they set off to Redbrook.

Bracing yourself for the worst, you try to keep a safe distance and listen for any distress but surprisingly, you hear no outcry in the distance.

You reach the outskirts of Redbrook where some town guards are inspecting incoming goods.

“...cabbage slugs?”

“Yes sir, it’s a very serious infestation from south of the Sinking Swamps in Tirfindoir. I’m going to need to check your cabbages.”

It’s at this moment you notice a cloaked figure slinking away from Baba’s cart.

The guards begin inspecting Baba’s cart, and before you can blink, one of them shouts, “SLUG INFESTATION!”

Several guards immediately throw oil and fire on Baba’s cart and set it alight while Baba cries out.

“MY CABBAGES!!!!!!”

name: My Cabbages VIII
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box; 1000x Gold Feather; 1000x Bucket; 1000x Gold Eggplant
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice; 250x Arnold Palmer
description:
A beleaguered Baba Gec sits dejectedly on the side of the road. You can see his resolve fading with each and every passing moment. You step forward, trying to summon some deep insight to help your newfound compatriot when the cloaked figure steps forward and whispers something in Baba’s ear.

Baba looks back and forth for a moment, his eyes narrowing. The cloaked figure steps away and heads towards the city market. Baba stands up and heads into the town proper and towards the post office. You are unsure by this sudden change in circumstances and decide to head home. Upon arriving, you find a letter.

You’ve been such an invaluable help, {player}. Though this town seems to conspire against me, I will not be deterred. I know these cabbages have so much value to this town and we’re gonna do this - together! If you could help me, I’ve discovered an invaluable resource to get our cabbages to market. We’ll just need the cabbages and a few…extra components.

Sincerely,

Baba Gec

Cabbage Dealer

name: My Cabbages IX
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
The following day you have cabbages piled high, with the oddly familiar extras in some buckets to the side. Baba strolls up to your farm as Cecil keeps his stride effortlessly. Baba is as cheerful and charming as the first day you met. He’s talking animatedly to Cecil about the various benefits of cabbages to digestive tracts, and their intensely vibrant additive flavor to bacon.

“You are a surprisingly knowledgeable merchant of cabbage, my friend. I look forward to seeing your sales tactics in Redbrook!”

Cecil and Baba greet you and without much pomp or circumstance, Cecil gathers the components and in a blinding golden flash, Cecil, Baba, and the crops have all disappeared.

You go back to your daily farm duties. In the back of your mind, you can't help but continue to think about the strange partnership of Baba and Cecil. Slowly your curiosity continues to build until you can no longer wait any longer, and you head into the city to see what's become of the curious cabbage dealer.

As you head towards Market Square you hear in the distance, “MY CABBAGES!!!”

Luckily you came prepared.

name: My Cabbages X
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box; 2000x Watermelon
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
“...They’re selling so well! And it’s all thanks to Cecil and {player}.”

Baba is positively beaming. The throngs of townsfolk around his humble cabbage cart are larger than you could have possibly imagined.

“...could you possibly bring more cabbages? This is why cabbages are the best!” Baba looks over to you and whispers, “But I hear melons are where the real action is at, maybe we should get some of those too?”

You can’t help but feel happy for Baba, and the pay is more than you are getting at the market, so you decide to take him up on his request.

name: My Cabbages XI
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box; 2000x Watermelon; 1000x Board; 1000x Nails; 1000x Hammer; 1000x Straw
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
You begin carting the cabbages and watermelons back to town the next day and, thanks to your vigilance, you see no threats on your journey in.

Your only stoppage on this trip was finding Mariya on the outskirts of town, foraging for some herbs.

“Oh, greetings {player}, it’s been a while.” You can’t remember the last time you talked with Mariya directly, and agree apologetically.

“I’m just scrounging for some new recipe ideas, the ones we have are great, but I’ve been thinking about trying something less common. If you have any ideas, let me know!”

You think on it for a moment, and promise to suggest any ideas that come your way and head towards town.

Baba excitedly unloads your cabbages and melons and begins arranging them around his cart.

“Today promises to be the best day yet! Sales are through the roof. In fact, I really need a roof, at this point. I am hoping to promote my cabbage-only diet today. That’ll be sure to bring the customers in! And melons. Boy - so many ideas. Sure they’re just organic husks of flavored water, but think of the potential! Let’s do another round for tomorrow. Also let me pay it forward, could you bring me some more supplies to improve my selling cart space?”

Baba has been honest with you so far, so you decide the pay is worth it and head back to gather what he needs.

name: My Cabbages XII
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box; 1000x Board; 1000x Nails; 1000x Hammer; 1000x Straw
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice; 5x Carved Bear
description:
Cart overflowing with watermelons, cabbages, and building supplies you head back into town.

Baba looks positively gleeful and takes all of your supplies. This time, Cecil is there as well as a gaggle of construction workers. It’s a surprising turn of events and you admit you feel suspicious of Cecil, who looks to be enjoying himself immensely helping supervise the controlled chaos.

One of the workers is raising a rather gaudy looking roof when you hear the sound of splintering wood. More splintering continues followed by a massive crack and snap - a support gives way, and the rafters come crashing down.

Cecil looks shocked before rushing into the dusty mess.

“MY CONSTRUCTION WORKERS!”

While Baba clutches his head in his hands.

"MY CABBAGES!"

name: My Cabbages XIII
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
The dust clears and it’s immediately obvious no one has been hurt, but Baba’s cabbage stock has been decimated by the collapse.

Cecil pulls you to the side with a relieved look on his face.

“Well, I guess if the only thing we’ve lost is some product, it’s a loss I’m willing to accept.”

Baba looks at Cecil in a mixed look of exasperation and disgust.

Baba turns to survey his ruined stock which is now lying crushed or soaking in a pool of dirty water from a broken pipe.

Going from dejected to positive in the space of a heartbeat Baba looks to Cecil - “If you can get this stand up and running, I might have a second revenue stream for us!”

You guess they’re going to need more cabbages.

name: My Cabbages XIV
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 1000x Corn; 1000x Onion; 500x Salt; 1000x Carrot; 1000x Potato; 1000x Leek; 1000x Tomato; 500x Bone Broth; 1000x Herbs; 1000x Rice; 1000x Cucumber; 1000x Mushroom; 1000x Seaweed; 1000x Sweet Root
rewardItems: 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice; 50x Grape Juice; 500x Arnold Palmer
description:
Baba’s Cabbage Shop has progressed nicely as you return. The roof has been rebuilt and reraised. As you look over it, the roof is somehow gaudier and more noticeable than the first iteration, with images of various vegetable leaves and produce carved into the shingling. In the center and rising out of the roof, leaves that resemble cabbages are the most prominent flourish. It's the strangest and most entrancing cabbage shack you've ever seen.

Baba has completely sold out of cabbages yet again on his ramshackle cart at the front of the building, with more townsfolk lining up for the next shipment. You notice his melon cart has seen very little interest, comparatively.

Baba pulls you to the side and conspiratorially looks at you, “Are you a good cook, or do you know one?”

Mariya immediately jumps to mind and you point Baba in her direction.

“Thanks friend! I just need a little advice. Maybe you could bring me some ingredients in the meantime for a new soup I dreamt up!”

name: My Cabbages XV
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 2000x Steak Kabob; 2000x Steak; 1000x Cheese; 1000x Milk; 1000x Flour; 1000x Wheat; 2000x Peppers; 1000x Butter; 1000x Rice; 1000x Bacon
rewardItems: 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice; 100x Engine; 25x Mega Cotton Seeds
description:
Baba’s new soup ingredients are delivered, and his excitement is palpable.

“This is going to be the greatest soup in the history of the wo-”

“Um actually Baba, a soup would be a dish that is primarily liquid, while your dish seems to more heavily favor the ingredients with just enough liquid to cover them, more colloquially known as stew.”

Mariya steps up beside Baba with a smile on her face and shakes your hand and greets you warmly.

“Oh hi, {player} it’s been a while! Baba here said you referred him to me and I'm so grateful to help another budding culinary specialist in Redbrook, and thank goodness it’s not pies for once! But…cabbages?” Her face contorts briefly as Baba looks at her.

“MY CABBAGES!!!!!!”

Mariya ignores Baba’s exclamation and suggests a few different options for their stew and asks if you could bring them back to her.

You bring some extra cabbages too, because it just feels right.

name: My Cabbages XVI
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 1000x Corn; 1000x Onion; 500x Salt; 1000x Carrot; 1000x Potato; 1000x Leek; 1000x Tomato; 500x Bone Broth; 1000x Herbs
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
Mariya is hard at work in her kitchen following Baba’s stew idea while sprinkling in a few of her own ideas at the same time. Each recipe comes out and Baba shakes his head each time.

“Too cabbagey. Not enough cabbage. Too many peppers. Milk? Blech! Syrup in stew?” You hear Baba utter a few more thoughts at the various ideas before an ear piercing scream, “MARIYA, MARIYAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA!!!”

Mariya comes blustering down the stairs, flour on her apron, and some dough across her cheek. “Yes chef?!” she blurts out.

Baba takes a step back and Mariya blushes fiercely, “...sorry it’s an old habit.”

“Well, so far none of these recipes have worked, is cabbage really this difficult to work with?”

“I think maybe we’ve gone too complicated Baba, let’s go back to the basics. What flavors do you really want to shine?”

Baba thinks for a moment before writing down his original list, but simpler and hands it to Mariya who simply nods in approval and passes you the list.

name: My Cabbages XVII
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 100x Fancy Table; 400x Fancy Chair; 2000x Spoon; 2000x Iron Cup
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice; 1x Lovely Cookies
description:
You return to Mariya’s kitchen, ingredients in tow. Over the next few hours, the strong smells of stewed cabbage reach your nose and entice your taste buds. Eventually there are eight different pots all stewing various recipes. Baba steps away after a few hours to check in on his shop which he left to Cecil during this process.

Baba returns after a few hours.

“My cabbages! They smell so delicious and they’re selling so well! Thanks {player} for being my original supplier. I hope we can work together more over the coming years to make Cabbage Corporation the greatest cabbage destination in the world! Speaking of which, we’re going to need more cabbages for the reveal of the stew. Can you get me some more cabbages that we can give away during the tasting? Yip yip! If you know anyone that can provide some modest furnishing, I’ll pay for that as well!”

name: My Cabbages XVIII
rewardItems: 500x Aquamarine; 500x Bucket; 500x Emberstone; 500x Coal; 50x Apple Cider; 200x Orange Juice
description:
You return to Cabbage Corp to find an elaborate display of stews presented, with some in various bowls, and spoons provided. The turnout is enormous, it seems like everyone is buying into the cabbage hype, much to your surprise.

The day proceeds without a hitch, Baba sells more cabbages than seems possible and everyone votes on their favorite stew.

Baba explains to the crowds, “Four of these stews are my personal creation and four of these are the creations of our good friend Mariya! Please vote on your favorite and it will become a mainstay of Cabbage Corp!”

The crowd shuffles through with compliments handed out all around. By the end of the day, everyone seems exhausted but satisfied and Baba, Mariya, and Cecil tally the votes together in the back.

Baba steps forward to announce the votes:

“In 8th place is Mariya’s Egg Cabbage Stew, in 7th place is Baba Gec’s Cheesy Cabbage Stew, in 6th place is Baba Gec’s Leek-splosion Cabbage Stew, in 5th place is Mariya’s Grape Cabbage Stew, in 4th place is Baba Gec’s Cabbage Cabbage and More Cabbage Stew, in Third place is Mariya’s Summer Selection Cabbage Surprise Stew, in 2nd place is Mariya’s Seasoned Cabbage Stew which means… I won? I won! Baba Gec’s Plain Old Cabbage Stew!”

Mariya enthusiastically shakes Baba’s hand and congratulates him. “You can be my wing-chef anytime, chef!” She waves gratefully to the few who stayed for the results and steps away.

Baba smiles widely and motions for you to sit down. “Well, this went far better than I expected, maybe you could bring me one more shipment of cabbages? I’ve already got some ideas from a local shipman that wants to bring cabbages from all over the world, but truth be told, yours have been my favorite so far and the townsfolk seem to agree. So, whaddya say?”

name: My Cabbages XIX
description:
You return with your bulk shipment of cabbages when you hear an uproar coming from the town square. You leave the cart on the outskirts of the square to find Buddy and Vincent once again sparring, this time with George joining in the shenanigans. Remembering your first encounter you quickly head back to the cart and move towards Baba’s shop. As you arrive Baba looks relieved.

“Thank goodness, I was worried you were waylaid by those hooligans in the town square. As thanks, I just want you to have my original cabbage recipe. You always stuck by me and helped. Cabbage Corporation will be forever grateful for your help. Let me know if you ever need anything, and I’ll do the same, eh?”

name: My Cabbages XX
itemsRequired: 2000x Cabbage; 4x Wagon Wheel; 2000x Wooden Plank; 1000x Wooden Box
description:
You take the slip of the recipe gratefully and walk away.

As you walk away you hear, “Wait! That’s it! A cabbage farm! They’ll all be MY CABBAGES!”

name: A Celebration of Freedom I
rewardItems: 25x Lemonade; 50x Orange Juice; 25x Apple; 25x Fishing Net
description: Rosalie wants to help Lorn commemorate this day of freedom and helping others. She’s planning a big party for the townsfolk to celebrate and feast, after Lorn’s morning of service activities. Will you help with a few things for the party?

name: A Celebration of Freedom II
rewardItems: 100x Lemonade; 100x Orange Juice; 100x Apple; 100x Fishing Net
description: Rosalie wants to add a few small fireworks for the celebration. She would love to go bigger with the fireworks, and so would Beatrix, but Lorn wants to keep it more quiet and more about conversations and coming together. Will you bring her a few bottle rockets?

name: A Celebration of Freedom III
rewardItems: 250x Orange Juice; 50x Arnold Palmer; 50x Large Net; 5x Large Chest 02; 7x Treasure Key
description: Rosalie wants to add a shrimp appetizer that Lorn loves as part of the celebration feast. Will you help her gather some of the ingredients?

name: A Celebration of Freedom IV
rewardItems: 500x Orange Juice; 100x Arnold Palmer; 100x Large Net; 1x Borgen Buck; 1x Lovely Cookies
description: Rosalie needs a few more food and drink items to add as choices for the feast. Will you bring a main dish and a drink, in quantities to share of course?

name: A Celebration of Freedom V
rewardItems: 500x Orange Juice; 500x Arnold Palmer; 500x Large Net; 1x Buddy Loves You Card; 1x Thank You Card; 1x Diamonds; 1x Hearts
description: To finish off the celebration feast, Rosalie already has several dessert options, but she would love to have some pie available. Will you bring some Red Berry Pie?

name: A Pearl Jam I
rewardItems: 200x Ancient Coin; 10x Arnold Palmer; 20x Grab Bag 01; 1x Thank You Card; 10x Grab Bag 06; 1x Lovely Cookies; 1x Gravy Boat
description:
When Holger was very young, his grandmother would pay Holger and Borgen a fat pouch of ancient coins each to go and explore all around the area in search of pearl berries. Holger and Borgen usually only filled a single basket after several long days of exploring. These extremely rare berries were very difficult to find, but, according to Holger, the taste was not to be found anywhere else.

Holger also told Rosalie about some of the recipes that his grandmother created with the pearl berries. They were all delicious, but Holger’s absolute favorite was the pearl berry jam.

All of these stories from Holger made Rosalie excited to taste the pearl berries and to try them in her cooking. Unfortunately, she can’t find a single berry.

Will you help Rosalie search, and if you find any berries, will you bring a couple for her to taste?

name:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty I
itemsRequired: 6000x Aquamarine Ring; 50x Breakfast Boost; 6000x Crossbow; 6000x Emerald Ring; 3000x Fancy Drum; 2000x Fancy Table; 500x Flywheel; 2000x Frost Shield; 6000x Garnet Ring; 2000x Giant Squid; 500x Grape Juice; 25x Mushroom Stew; 6000x Mystic Ring; 750x Orange Gecko; 25x Over The Moon; 3000x Pear Grease; 6000x Pie Pan; 1000x Potato Battery; 6000x Purple Bag; 1000x Red Trunk; 1000x Rice; 6000x Ruby Ring; 50x Ship in a Bottle; 500x Small Chest 01; 1000x Spiked Shell; 2000x Steel Vise; 3000x Treasure Chest; 3000x Wizard Hat
rewardItems: 1x Ramjoram's Mask; 10x Cabbage Stew; 4000x Large Net
questline:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty
description:
In a sound all too familiar, feathers shower your floor and Star Meerif appears, sitting on a fancy chair, face impassive and unreadable.

“Greetings {player}, it has been a while. I’ve missed our interactions quite a lot, and despite appearances, I’m quite fond of both your help and your tenacity. I’ve run into a problem and I’m looking to you for help.”

Star stands up and begins pacing while looking thoughtfully towards you every few moments.

“Can I tell you a secret? You see, magic is funny. My mentor used to tell me that magic was ten percent luck, twenty percent skill, fifteen percent concentrated power of will, five percent pleasure, and sixty percent unexplained. Obviously it’s more complicated than that, but the reality is sometimes you need real effort to power magic. It’s not just about specific coloration or gems, but their transmatrical energy is absolutely an important part of a spell's efficacy. Modern magical theory really struggles to explain the antimatter resonances between aligning matrices and aligning the energy investiture that is passed from naturally occurring sources of cranial application. In “T’rah: A History,” there have been several notable wizards who started the study of investiture of non-matter energy, mainly Dansernos in 223 and Byrer in 447…”

You begin to nod off as Star continues in detail about T’rah and magical theory. Deciding enough is enough, you step away to cook some breakfast as Star continues on, pacing the entire time.

“{player}, I’m stuck! I need help! I…we…won that treasure map in exciting fashion. No one ever considered hiding so many flags on a planeshifted dimensional warp between light and dark, but even now I’ve no idea what the map means!”

Star produces an identical copy of your treasure map and guffaws as you produce the same map.

“Oh, I made a copy, sorry. Anyways, let me sum up the problem. There’s a magical energy hidden in this map but I need to match it’s transmatrical energy and I realized there is so much effort put into this map I can’t possibly do it alone. I was hoping you could help me match the emanations of investment from the map and we could find the treasure together. I’ve been working on this so long that I will do my best to reward you in excess of the time and energy you’ve spent here, afterall it’s been almost an entire branch I’ve spent on this problem!”

Star hands you a list of items and you quickly realize he was not kidding about the amount of work this was going to take.

name:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty II
itemsRequired: 6000x Aquamarine Ring; 6000x Blue Purse; 50x Breakfast Boost; 1000x Corn Oil; 6000x Crab Claw; 6000x Crossbow; 6000x Emerald Ring; 3000x Fancy Chair; 3000x Fancy Guitar; 500x Flywheel; 6000x Garnet Ring; 2000x Giant Squid; 500x Grape Juice; 2500x Machine Press; 25x Mushroom Stew; 6000x Mystic Ring; 25x Over The Moon; 3000x Pear Grease; 6000x Pie Pan; 1000x Red Shield; 1000x Rice; 6000x Ruby Ring; 500x Small Chest 01; 2000x Steel Vise; 3000x Treasure Chest; 3000x Wizard Hat
questline:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty
description:
Star takes all of the things you’ve brought him and moves them into unique piles. He mutters some words and the space around the items begins to shimmer. You watch as one by one each pile contracts and warps the space around it. Blinking your eyes, the sight makes you feel a little woozy and after a moment, only a handful of treasure chests remain on the ground. “The old Chest of Holding trick, learned this one from a book, but the subpixel alignment was tricky…” He pauses a moment before looking at you, “Well, guess we’ll have to take these back by hand. Next time we’ll add feathers to the mix and speed up the lattice transference. And I just love featherfall.”

Star’s tower is as majestic as it is plain and uniquely magical. Surfaces glitter in the light, and there is a seam in the middle from the damage Geist inflicted to the tower in the past, but that only seems to add to the mystique and beauty of the building. Crystal parapets reflect sunlight, glittering rooftops shimmer in extraordinary ways reacting to unseen inputs, a direct contrast to the staid concrete walls and windows. You are directed towards the basement and with each step you descend, the walls widen until you reach a room that is clearly bigger than the entirety of the base of the tower, which your brain struggles to rationalize.

Star arranges the treasure chests in a circle and with a wave of his hand, they empty their contents, a rush of power and air sweeping by you as all the items expand back to their previous sizes. Star stands in the middle of the room, the treasure map laid before him, a single ring from each of the rings you brought to him plucked from the pile, and a double inset ring in the middle.

He stretches his arms forth and you can sense a tingle in the back of your mind. The magical energy in the basement is palpable, as invisible runes suddenly come to life, lining the walls in a dazzling display of color.

As quickly as it begins, the runes fade, and a single speck of light falls from between Star’s outstretched arms, and lands on the map, causing a flare of color.

Star laughs with glee at the sight. “I knew it! The transference of rarity, effort and talent all combined to help power this spell of finding! {player}, we need more! More! I think we need to adjust the expenditure slightly, so I’ve made some modifications to your list. Hopefully we’ll get a clearer picture of what that map is hiding next time. Oh, and, thanks {player}.”

name:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty III
itemsRequired: 6000x Aquamarine Ring; 50x Breakfast Boost; 3000x Butter; 200x Concord Grape Pie; 6000x Crab Claw; 6000x Crossbow; 6000x Emerald Ring; 3000x Fancy Drum; 2000x Fancy Table; 500x Flywheel; 6000x Garnet Ring; 2000x Giant Squid; 6000x Gold Leaf; 500x Grape Juice; 6000x Jade Charm; 25x Mushroom Stew; 6000x Mystic Ring; 750x Orange Gecko; 25x Over The Moon; 3000x Pear Grease; 6000x Pie Pan; 1000x Potato Battery; 6000x Purple Bag; 1000x Red Trunk; 1000x Rice; 6000x Ruby Ring; 100x Ship in a Bottle; 500x Small Chest 01; 2000x Steel Vise; 3000x Treasure Chest; 3000x Wizard Hat
rewardItems: 1x Ramjoram's Mask; 2000x Apple Cider; 5000x Orange Juice; 100x Raptor Claw
questline:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty
description:
You return to your home, and Star greets you warmly. As he gathers your materials in a similar fashion to before, he spreads the feathers around the outer bounds of the items. “A circle is a powerful tool in the hands of a skilled practitioner.” He motions you to step into the circle and Star pulls a staff out of thin air and begins to speak.

“This is gonna be a little wild. TEL-”

The world rushes around you and you feel as though you’re being pulled into a tiny string. The world elongates around you as shapes and colors rush past you, distorted and wonderful and awful all at the same time.

“EDORUS!”

As suddenly as it begins, you find yourself in the basement of Star’s tower.

“What a rush! I’ve never done a side-along with a non-magic user before, it worked out well! Now you know why I love that spell so much!”

You nod tentatively as you try to regain your senses and composure.

“Anyways, let’s do a little more power transference with a hint of finding magic this time, shall we?”

Star arranges the myriad of items once again and casts his spell. You see another flicker of light falling from Star’s hands to the map, this time revealing a small line.

Star whoops with glee and immediately hands you another list.

He smiles, “I’d say we’re about 50% of the way there, and I’m so very grateful for your help! I promise to etch your name into the hall of findings once we’re done.”

name:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty IV
itemsRequired: 6000x Aquamarine Ring; 6000x Blue Purse; 50x Breakfast Boost; 3000x Butter; 1000x Corn Oil; 6000x Crossbow; 6000x Emerald Ring; 3000x Fancy Chair; 3000x Fancy Guitar; 500x Flywheel; 2000x Frost Shield; 6000x Garnet Ring; 2000x Giant Squid; 500x Grape Juice; 25x Mushroom Stew; 6000x Mystic Ring; 25x Over The Moon; 3000x Pear Grease; 6000x Pie Pan; 200x Red Berry Pie; 1000x Red Shield; 1000x Rice; 6000x Ruby Ring; 500x Small Chest 01; 1000x Spiked Shell; 2000x Steel Vise; 3000x Treasure Chest; 3000x Wizard Hat
rewardItems: 2000x Arnold Palmer; 2x Lovely Cookies; 3x Lemon Cream Pie
questline:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty
description:
You follow the same process as last time. On your second teleportation trip, you swear you see a reflection of yourself as you are stretched and smashed. The treasure map gleams a little brighter after the spell and Star addresses you frankly.

“So, I’ve definitely underestimated the amount of effort and investitudiny this spell is going to take. We’ll need to crank up some more rare items and have some lowered expectation for effort versus rewards for a bit. I, however, am grateful for your help and hope to reward you for your effort more cleanly than this spell is doing. And who knows what we’ll find at the end of this map.”

You get the odd feeling something isn’t quite right. When have you ever seen a spell from Star ever take this much…work?

name:
Starting To Actually Realize
Magic Ain't Pretty V
